programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A hospital patient recovering from a hit-and-run incident dies in suspicious circumstances, but a delirious homeless woman who believes she has witnessed God performing a mercy killing could hold the key to the murder investigation. First-ever episode of the detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark treats a beautiful amnesiac, unaware she is actually a hired assassin who has infiltrated the hospital to kill influential senator Robert Bassett, who has been admitted for surgery. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble learns a one-armed man has been arrested and risks capture to see if he is the man who killed his wife

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A sculptor returns from Greece with a beautiful model, but when her disapproving mother is found dead, he is charged with murder and Perry must defend him.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman tracks down a hijacked ambulance containing a donor organ. Dionne Warwick guest stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio becomes prime suspect in a murder investigation, and makes matters worse for himself by admitting he not only dated the victim, but was also the last person to see her alive. As events unfold, the detective realises he is being framed by a psychopath he caught in New York, but could not find sufficient evidence to convict

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.desc: Stella investigates a sexual assault in Central Park, a case hampered by the fact that the attacker left no DNA at the scene. Meanwhile, Mac and Aiden try to find the killer of a drug dealer - only to discover the fatal bullet has been swallowed by a rat. Starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Michele Hicks and Vanessa Ferlito

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A practical joke turns into a murder mystery when a skeleton found on a tour bus turns out to be authentic. Mac and Stella are given the task of identifying the victim, but the evidence points to two periods in Times Square's history, 20 years apart. With Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter is forced to enlist the help of former troubled team member and genius Mark Collins when a nuclear reactor at a Los Angeles power plant is on the verge of a cataclysmic meltdown. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members must pass a mandated psychiatric evaluation or face being disbanded after they are accused of destroying a priceless work of art. Meanwhile, Ralph is reluctant to go to his school's Halloween party. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Jenny Shepard tries to solve the murder of an underworld source who was prepared to give information on the activities of arms dealer La Grenouille. Unfortunately her behaviour becomes increasingly obsessive as the case proceeds, leaving Gibbs wondering if her judgement is being clouded by a personal motivation. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A blind man unwittingly photographs evidence of a murder, leading Jethro and the team to the corpse of a petty officer who was stabbed with a knife left at the scene. Further evidence indicates his death was not the result of a random mugging - but cracking the case proves difficult. Meanwhile, Tony has relationship problems. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is made acting director while Jenny is attending an overseas conference. However, he decides to ignore his duties and investigate the murder of a man who arrived dead in a taxi at the team's headquarters with no clues to his identity - except a mysterious list of names. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs tries to find out why the agents are being forced by Homeland Security to take polygraph tests, while Tony and Jeanne are held hostage by a drug dealer. Elsewhere, Jenny returns from Europe to discover she has had a surprise visitor. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: As the hunt for La Grenouille intensifies, Tony's cover is blown and his car is later destroyed in an explosion, leaving the team convinced he has been killed. However, Ducky's autopsy on the body of the driver reveals it was actually someone else who died in the blast

programme.desc: A bomb detonates at a military charity concert, killing two men. Meanwhile, Jimmy prepares for parenthood. Keith Carradine guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gibbs joins forces with the FBI when the body of a congressman and former agent washes ashore in New Orleans. Together they try to determine if the murder could be linked to a well-known case the victim had previously worked on during his career. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. DiNozzo, McGee and the FBI continue to track political connections to a case, and Bishop sends DiNozzo a souvenir. Meanwhile, Gibbs and Special Agent Pride chase leads in New Orleans after evidence points to a copycat killer. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee's girlfriend Delilah makes a breakthrough in a case involving the alleged death of a US navy lieutentant. However, when her boss orders her to back away, she turns to the NCIS team for help. Meanwhile, McGee talks to DiNozzo about whether he should move forward with his relationship. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.desc: Paranormal drama inspired by the life of medium Allison DuBois. Patricia Arquette stars as a woman plagued by visions of the dead, who uses her unique gift to solve murder cases. However, she comes into conflict with a cynical ranger who is sceptical about her ability

programme.title: Mission: Impossible

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the case of a building worker who died in an underground explosion. An X-ray reveals about 200 of the victim's bones are broken and the autopsy shows he succumbed to asthmatic asphyxiation before the blast. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden examine the death of a teenager found strangled and floating in a creek

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Danny and Aiden investigate the case of employees of a Brooklyn cafe found dead with plastic bags over their heads. As events unfold, a discovery is made about the type of duct tape used on the victims which could help track down the killer. Meanwhile, Mac and Stella set out to locate the body of a man whose severed lower leg has turned up in an alleyway

Meanwhile, a member of the Baltimore police department helping with the case appears to have a grudge against Tony

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

Instead she phones an investigation company hired by the child's family to look for him - and is introduced to their employee Cynthia Keener. With guest star Anjelica Huston, alongside Patricia Arquette

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison helps Cynthia Keener hunt for a missing teenage girl, while Ariel is asked to attend a concert without any chaperones, and finds the invitation somehow gives her unusual dreams. Drama starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison investigates a girl's disappearance - a case that seems to have a strange bearing on her dreams of Paris. As an added inducement, she is aware that finding the missing youngster could help Joe get a job. Drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison loses her hearing while investigating the abduction of a deaf 12-year-old, forcing Joe to help solve the case. programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.desc: Maria is determined to have the operation, despite Donna and her parents' misgivings and her limited chance of survival. programme.title: The Bill

Emma reveals a maverick streak and puts her career on the line to help a reformed drug addict stay out of jail

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Frank can't understand why business is so bad, while David seems surprisingly unperturbed. Rumours are rife about the new market inspector and Robbie has a bad experience

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol and Alan get a new lodger. Michael Rose makes his presence felt. Roy consoles Pat, while Frank finds he is being pushed to the limit

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel, starring Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha. A wealthy eligible bachelor rents a property in Hertfordshire, causing a flurry of anticipation among the ladies of the district. However, it transpires he also brought along a friend - the haughty and aloof Fitzwilliam Darcy

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Elizabeth visits Mr Collins once Charlotte has accepted his proposal of marriage and confides in Jane that she regards him as one of the stupidest men in England. Mrs Bennet sizes up George Wickham as a potential suitor for her daughter - but he has set his heart on society heiress Miss King

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Despite Miss Bingley's efforts to keep them apart, Darcy and Elizabeth's relationship grows more affectionate - until bad news arrives from Longbourn about Lydia and Wickham. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Jennifer Ehle, Colin Firth and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Wickham's elopement with Lydia makes Elizabeth see him for what he really is, while Bingley proposes to Jane - an offer she readily accepts. Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Famous thriller author Frank O'Toole is found stabbed through the heart after taking an early morning swim in the ocean. Suspicion soon falls on the victim's wife Valerie, but with eyewitnesses confirming he was swimming alone, how could anyone have got from the house to the sea without being detected? Away from the investigation, Dwayne concentrates on finding a date for a voodoo love festival. Whodunit, guest starring Simon Callow, Imogen Stubbs, and Sian Gibson of Car Shame fame

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens a 20-year-old case when a camera and film belonging to a photographic lab assistant murdered in 1987 are found by chance in a Soho pub toilet. New evidence leads the detectives into the world of modelling and to the door of a professional footballer. Meanwhile, Brian's erratic behaviour takes an alarming turn, and Gerry is less than happy when his daughter Emily (played by Dennis Waterman's real-life daughter Hannah) joins the team and looks to Sandra as a role model

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry kidnaps Jim Coaver and interrogates him about the recent attacks, but he soon realises the CIA deputy-director could be innocent. Before he can plan his next move, the Americans arrive to liberate their boss, at which point Coaver gives his old friend one final clue - vital evidence can be found on a laptop at the US Embassy, requiring Harry's team to steal the computer from under the noses of their fellow spies

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry faces deportation to America, so he agrees to go quietly on the condition that the signing of the alliance between the UK and Russia be brought forward. But when Elena confides in Ruth that she has information about an imminent attack on the UK, Section D have no choice but to break their boss out of CIA custody. With all the main players assembled, the stage is set for a highly dramatic climax to the long-running spy drama

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Former Casualty actress Georgina Bouzova guest stars as a rape victim whose plight prompts Jack to reopen a cold case from 20 years previously, with help from an old colleague. Emma reveals a maverick streak and puts her career on the line to help a reformed drug addict stay out of jail

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A party of pilgrims is imprisoned within Shrewsbury Abbey on suspicion of murdering an old man whose body is discovered in a sack. Sleuthing monk Cadfael is soon on the scent of the killer, boiling the corpse down to its bones to ascertain the cause of death - and finding a tiny cross that could provide a vital clue. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Julian Firth and Anthony Green

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A body is found in an ANC drinking den and Gene quickly decides a girl is guilty of the murder. However, the group's leader complicates matters by confessing to the crime to stop the suspect being deported, pitching the team into a world of illegal immigration and terrorism. Jim's report is nearly finished, but he continues to push Alex to find out what really happened to Sam Tyler

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: With the team's attention focused on investigating a diamond robbery and the murder of three gang members, Alex slips away, vowing to learn the truth about Gene Hunt. When the DCI realises what she is up to, he sets off in pursuit, knowing her discoveries will destroy his world. Final episode of the fantasy crime drama, starring Philip Glenister, Keeley Hawes, Dean Andrews and Marshall Lancaster

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Framed by long-time enemy Ducos for stealing Napoleon's treasures, Sharpe is abandoned by his wife Jane, and goes on the run from both the English and the French until he can prove his innocence. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The group arrives in Germany with hopes of high wages and bierkellers full of frauleins, but the truth is much harder to bear. Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais's popular comic drama following the fortunes of English workers seeking their fortune overseas, starring Tim Healy, Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Neville is suspended for lying about being a joiner, but manages to redeem himself by averting a disaster on the site. He is not only reinstated by Herr Ulrich, but allowed to work as a bricklayer - which is bad news for Oz, who is sacked to make way for him. Drama, starring Tim Healy, Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Oz finds himself back in England after a night of heavy drinking - but has no idea how he got there. Tim Healy, Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Horticultural lecturer Rosemary Boxer is hired by a wealthy but ill businessman to help cure a disease causing his trees to lose their bark. She gets to know Laura Thyme, who turns up after a friend is killed in a suspicious car accident, and when the pair find the lord of the manor unconscious beneath his bedroom window, they must decide whether depression led him to attempt suicide or if there is a more sinister explanation. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal, Pam Ferris, David Mallinson and Roger Alborough

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The amateur sleuths uncover the remains of a racehorse while engaged on horticultural work in the grounds of pop star Nev Connolly's supposedly haunted mansion. Before long, gruesome revelations are coming thick and fast as Nev is shot dead and more bones are discovered - this time belonging to a young woman. As they investigate further, Rosemary and Laura are plunged into a sinister mystery with roots deep in the past. Drama, guest starring Murray Head (Heartbeat) and Abigail Cruttenden (Benidorm)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd is called upon to investigate when a rugby coach's body is found stripped naked and tied to a goal post. His team's losing streak of 50 straight games has broken national records, and Shepherd is perplexed by a sense of relief in the community. Starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: A street urchin hires Phryne to find his missing brother, leading to a sinister trail of dead boys. Hugh wrestles with career and engagement problems

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of a murder victim who was killed with an axe is dredged from the bottom of the Clyde. The discovery sparks an investigation which takes Jim and his officers away from the mean streets of Glasgow into the scenic Scottish Highlands. Police drama, starring Mark McManus and James MacPherson, and guest starring Peter Mullan

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jim investigates the murders of a Glasgow by-election candidate and her agent, who had been championing the return of capital punishment. However, the veteran detective finds himself hard-pressed to arrive at a quick solution to the crime. Elsewhere, a young woman is strangled by her stalker then raped. Robert Carlyle makes one of his earliest screen appearances, alongside star Mark McManusrs

programme.title: Taggart

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis's girlfriend nurse Tash Niles is attacked in the hospital car park, raising the terrifying prospect that Ray Moore is targeting people who could testify against him. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A road accident victim (former Hear'Say singer Suzanne Shaw) is found alone in the wreck of her car, but Crime Scene Examiner Lorna Hart is convinced a second person was in the vehicle at the time of the accident. As Rudy's condition deteriorates, Tash finally agrees to testify against Ray Moore, but the case isn't moving fast enough for Lewis, who takes matters into his own hands

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As Lewis's fingerprints are found all over the gun next to Ray Moore's body, the young PC heads for the station to make a statement and hand in his warrant card, while Heaton regrets losing the chance to bring his old enemy to justice. New recruit DC Grace Dasari (This Life's Amita Dhiri) teams up with Mickey to help a woman trace her son, who has been missing for 11 years

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Adaptation of Victor Hugo's novel, which follows an epic journey through 19th-century France as the nation stands on the brink of revolution. Dominic West plays the lead role of Jean Valjean, a former convict attempting to begin his life anew while all around him a desperate underclass struggles to survive. Valjean's search for a fresh start is thwarted by prejudice against ex-convicts and the suspicion of an obsessed former guard who is convinced he will reoffend. Elsewhere, an army officer returns home from the battle of Waterloo desperate to reconnect with his son, while a seamstress has an encounter that could lead to her downfall. With David Oyelowo, Lily Collins and David Bradley

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: A desperately poor Fantine is forced to flee Paris in search of work, and is left contemplating a terrible decision that could ensure her daughter enjoys a brighter future. Her fate takes an even darker turn as she struggles to meet the increasingly outrageous demands of the Thenardiers. Valjean settles into his new life as a prosperous factory owner, but his newfound happiness is threatened when Javert is appointed as the town's new police inspector, and remains as determined as ever to expose him

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Jean Valjean is tormented by his conscience and, under relentless pressure from Javert, is forced to confess his true identity. However, his life looks is changed entirely once again due to a promise he makes to Fantine to keep Cosette safe. The Thenardiers see a bright future ahead when a wealthy stranger seeks lodgings at their inn - but their joy is doomed to be short-lived. Javert rises to become a police officer in Paris, and pursues a new lead involving a report of a missing girl that could finally allow him to bring Valjean down

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Cosette has grown into a teenager, and to Valjean's dismay, questions her closeted life and is desperate for a new start in Paris. The opportunity presents itself when she meets Pontmercy's son Marius, who has turned his back on his family after learning the truth about his father, and now lives in a squalid tenement, where he catches the eye of the daughter of a criminal family. Javert ascends through the ranks of the police, and believes he is closing in on his goal

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Paris stands on the brink of revolt during the funeral of General Lamarque. Enjolras leads a group of protesting students and workers to construct a barricade in the streets, while an increasingly desperate Valjean fears for Cosette's safety and arranges for her to go into hiding as Javert's search for them grows ever more relentless. Marius scours the city for the missing Cosette, assisted by a heartbroken Eponine - but when he cannot find her, he is left consumed with despair and decides to join Enjolras at the forefront of the uprising

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: The army prepares to make another attack on the barricades as the fighting grows more vicious and desperate. Valjean feels conflicted about Marius' relationship with Cosette and faces a heartbreaking choice when he comes to realise what is in her best interests. However, he underestimates the strength of Cosette's feelings for him. Javert is thrown into turmoil by the selfless actions of a man he has always viewed as an irredeemable criminal, and is forced to reconsider everything he believes in

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alvin dresses as a crow, but finds it difficult to walk in a straight line, and the disguise gives Howard an idea as he tries to hide from Pearl. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton, Brian Murphy and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest sets out to protect an old friend by investigating a 300-year-old secret hidden in a university. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: First of a two-part story by Lynda La Plante in which the team investigate the death of a prostitute who plunged from the balcony of a block of luxury apartments. They discover she was the daughter of a famous Seventies TV star murdered 12 years previously, whose killer was never traced, and try to find a link between the two crimes. David Hayman stars as the newly promoted DCS Michael Walker, alongside Victoria Smurfit, Colin Salmon and Frances Barber

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Concluding part. Jonathan Southwood is taken in for questioning, during which a series of shocking revelations point to the true murderer. Walker faces a race against time to reach Connor, who is working undercover at a private club where Colin Thorpe is putting her professionalism to the ultimate test. Crime drama, starring Victoria Smurfit, David Hayman and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry VIII is desperate to end his marriage to Katherine of Aragon, but Cardinal Wolsey's efforts to persuade the pope to grant an annulment are fruitless - much to the dismay of Thomas Cromwell, a lawyer dependent on Wolsey's patronage. The king's new mistress is rumoured to be Anne Boleyn, who has sworn vengeance on Wolsey after he refused to let her marry Harry Percy, so Cromwell visits her in the hope of swaying her to the cardinal's side. Historical drama adapted by Peter Straughan from Hilary Mantel's Booker Prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, and starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Heno
programme.desc: An entertaining mix bidding farewell to the strange year of 2020. There's a special song by Geraint Lovgreen, one of Wrexham's biggest fans, to celebrate the purchase of the club by two of Hollywood's biggest stars. The Cabarela crew have two new songs looking back over the year, and we enjoy an emotional song from some fabulous Welsh West End stars, led by Steffan Rhys Hughes. Plus, a special interview with world-renowned star Matthew Rhys and contributions from Rhys Meirion, Aled Hall and Trystan Llyr Griffiths

The last one to be scared 'Shrekless' will be declared the winner. Animation based on the film series, featuring the voices of Mike Myers and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: The Boss Baby (2017)
programme.desc: A youngster believes his baby brother is actually a power-hungry tycoon sent to destroy his perfect family life. However, the duo must put aside their differences and work together when they uncover a plot to sabotage the company their parents work for. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Alec Baldwin, Steve Buscemi, Jimmy Kimmel and Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's second children's book about the accident-prone dragon. Zog is helping his friend Princess Pearl to provide much-needed medical care to all manner of creatures, assisted by knight Sir Gadabout. However, when a storm forces them to return to Pearl's home at the palace, her uncle the king does not approve of her career - at least until he falls ill himself. Narrated by Lenny Henry, with the voices of Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Archbishop of Canterbury's New Year Message

programme.desc: A young meerkat queen lives in the harsh beauty of the Madgadikgadi salt pans. If her new pups are to survive, she must unite her family in the face of rivals, predators and immense dust storms. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World (2019)
programme.desc: Viking warrior Hiccup and his faithful dragon Toothless venture out into a hidden world to protect their village from the darkest threat yet. Third chapter in the animated fantasy saga, featuring the voices of Jay Baruchel, America Ferrera and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming (2019)
programme.desc: A decade after events of How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World, brave warrior Hiccup and faithful sidekick Toothless reunite. They attempt to remind both their kinds of the inseparable bond between Vikings and dragons as they prepare for the Snoggletog Festival. Animated drama, with the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves (1991)
programme.desc: English nobleman Robin of Locksley returns home from fighting in the Crusades, only to discover that his father has been murdered and his lands seized, while the local people are living in fear under the tyrannical rule of the Sheriff of Nottingham. Branded an outlaw for his defiance, he and his Moorish travelling companion go into hiding in the forest, where they recruit a group of bandits to fight for justice, protect the downtrodden, and foil the Sheriff's plan to seize the throne while the king is away at war. Medieval action adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Alan Rickman, Morgan Freeman, Mary Elizabeth Mastrantonio, Christian Slater and Mike McShane

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is locked away in a high-security alien prison. Isolated, alone, with no hope of escape. Far away, on Earth, her best friends, Yaz, Ryan and Graham, have to pick up their lives without her. However, it is not easy as old habits die hard - especially when they discover a disturbing plan forming. A plan that involves a Dalek. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh and John Barrowman

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda plans for her future, while Mick prepares to confront his deepest fears head on - but when the moment arrives, will he be able to go through with it? Stacey, Kat and Martin are worried about Kush, Stuart finds himself in trouble, and Jay snaps at Ben over comments about his love life. Patrick goes through with a deal, but it may prove costly

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Eight-part drama inspired by real events, telling the remarkable story of the capture of the French fraudster and killer, Charles Sobhraj. When a junior diplomat at the Dutch Embassy in Bangkok unwittingly walks into Charles Sobhraj's intricate web of crime, he sets off an extraordinary chain of events as he attempts to bring Sobhraj to justice for his crimes. Starring Tahar Rahim, Jenna Coleman, Billy Howle and Ellie Bamber

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy plan some New Year's Eve festivities in this Christmas special, with both inviting their parents to join them. Things soon go badly wrong as party games give way to an uncompromising round of guests pointing out what New Year's resolutions everyone needs to make - with a distinct lack of festive spirit on display. Starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching He-Huang and Ben Tish, and special guest Grace Dent. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to create a garden incorporating a BMX track for youngster Ollie, who has yet to reach the age of 10. Oliver's family, from Appleton in Cheshire, has budgeted £3,000 for the budget, and it's going to need every penny of that to successfully transform the present bland suburban space into a cross between a race track for Oliver and a show garden for parents Sally and Ian

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Chelsea v Manchester City and Southampton v Liverpool in the Premier League

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets a photographer attempting to capture images of the UK's remaining art deco cinemas

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple who hope to realise their dream of escaping to Devon. They have a generous budget of £1.4million, and hope to find a property with land, a self-contained annex and lots of room for guests. Away from country homes, Jules visits a sustainable family farm producing food in harmony with nature

programme.title: Big Hero 6 (2014)
programme.desc: A lonely, orphaned computer genius forms a special bond with an inflatable robot. When a devastating event occurs in their futuristic home city, they join forces with a group of friends to form a hi-tech band of heroes who are determined to get to the bottom of the matter. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Ryan Potter, Scott Adsit and Daniel Henney

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where items saved from the skip include a wooden clothes airer and a modern fire surround

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Poet Lemn Sissay, BBC Radio 1 DJ Arielle Free, actress Crissy Rock and sports broadcaster Andrew Cotter go under the spotlight tonight answering questions on Sylvia Pankhurst, Daft Punk, The Pendle Witch Trials and Smallfilms

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous names from radio take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Johnnie Walker, Sally Boazman, Stuart Maconie, Elizabeth Alker, Scott Mills, Chris Stark, Vick Hope and Harriet Scott

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Connie struggles to protect her team when the coronavirus pandemic hits Holby ED, Will becomes disillusioned with his work and Fenisha rushes to keep her baby safe

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy's sister, who has a reputation for wild living, arrives in time to throw the glamorous socialite's New Year party plans into disarray. Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley star, with guest Kate O'Mara

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Leeds United. The north London rivals involved in these fixtures have enjoyed contrasting fortunes this season, with Spurs near the top of the table, and Arsenal floundering in the lower reaches following a series of home defeats

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of the latest matches and look ahead to the concluding regular-season fixtures

programme.title: Internal Affairs (1990)
programme.desc: A crooked cop uses his badge and uniform as a cover for his unsavoury money-making schemes and is prepared to murder anyone who stands in his way. A zealous investigator vows to bring him to justice, but the cunning detective responds by turning his adversary's wife against him. Crime thriller, starring Richard Gere, Andy Garcia, Laurie Metcalf, Nancy Travis and William Baldwin

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Leeds United. The north London rivals involved in these fixtures have enjoyed contrasting fortunes this season, with Spurs near the top of the table, and Arsenal floundering in the lower reaches following a series of home defeats

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Henry Hull leads the ecumenical service for the Second Sunday of Christmas from Down Cathedral in Downpatrick, Co Down. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a large two-bedroom apartment in Battersea, south-west London, while Martin Roberts looks over plans to develop a house in Winsford, Cheshire

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross oversees oversees proceedings at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells as two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell on for profit. Experts Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant attempt to guide the sides to success at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores St Paul's Cathedral, with music including hymns sung by the Cathedral Choir and performances by Aled Jones and the Priests, and Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: Ranger Grace finds an orphaned 10-year-old boy in the forest, where he has been ever since the death of his parents five years earlier. What's more, he tells her he has been living with a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Could her father's tales of a mythical beast be true after all? Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Leamington Spa to help Robert and Gemma and their daughter Darcey, who is afraid of the family's nettle-strewn, overgrown garden

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vicar Steve Stewart and his wife Amanda bring their cherished rocking horse to the barn. The toy was a Christmas gift for their daughter Tamsin, who passed away at the age of seven. Also arriving at the barn is a treasured but tired stereogram, once the centrepiece of a family's festive celebrations, and a treasured toy truck made by a team of Second World War engineers

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: On the Town (1949)
programme.desc: Musical following three sailors who are out for a good time while on a day's shore leave in New York. One tries initially to fend off the advances of a female taxi driver, another is pulled into helping with an anthropologist's study of prehistoric man, while the third seeks out the model in a famous advertising campaign - but finds she is not quite what he expected. With Gene Kelly, Frank Sinatra and Jules Munshin

programme.title: New Year's Day Concert
programme.desc: The Vienna Philharmonic Orchestra, conducted by Riccardo Muti, perform their traditional New Year's Day concert, live from the Musikverein in Vienna. Narrated by Petroc Trelawny

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie takes a look at some of the leading ladies who have proved time and again, year after year, that they are amongst the very best of British talent

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: First of two special episodes of Bob Larbey's comedy, first shown in 2005. Lionel is dumbfounded by the delivery of two cots to the Hardcastle residence, given that there are no babies in the house. Jean explains that they are for their grandchildren, which is even more surprising given that Judith isn't even pregnant. Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer star

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Patrick searches for his father, Jean decides she wants grandchildren, Stephen aspires to be a celebrity and Lionel wants peace and quiet as well as a custard tart. Somehow, all of them inexplicably end up in hospital. Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer star in the second instalment of a two-part Christmas special first shown in 2005

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: The comedian and explorer revisits his fifth travel series - Himalaya. For Michael Palin, this epic journey across the world's highest mountain range is the fulfilment of boyhood dreams inspired by the 1953 ascent of Everest by Hillary and Tensing. Beginning in the famous Khyber Pass, he travels through Pakistan and India, ascends into Nepal and Tibet to reach Everest base camp itself

programme.title: The Archbishop of Canterbury's New Year Message
programme.desc: The Most Reverend Justin Welby looks ahead to the coming year and reflects on the importance of connecting with other people

programme.title: Mr Holmes (2015)
programme.desc: An elderly Sherlock Holmes retires to a seaside farmhouse in the aftermath of the Second World War, and struggles to cope with his diminished faculties. Assisted by his housekeeper's inquisitive son, he tries to piece together the events of the one case that he failed to solve. Period mystery based on Mitch Cullin's novel A Slight Trick of the Mind, starring Ian McKellen, Laura Linney and Milo Parker

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents a special music edition of the quiz show, which sees a team of festival goers take on a three members of the Nuestra Singers. The contestants put their lateral thinking to the test as they strive to make connections between things that at first do not appear to be linked at first

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the grand final, as teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the academic quiz's festive edition. Which team will be crowned Christmas University Challenge champions?

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: Having learned how to sidestep the conventional media machinery, celebrities acquired the tools take charge of their own narrative on social media. In 2016, actress Meghan Markle became Prince Harry's girlfriend. They publicly confirmed their relationship and swiftly denounced the press. Meanwhile, Donald Trump became US President, taking everything he'd learned from business and media to his role, including catchphrases and shock tactics. Ed Balls showed his moves on the Strictly dancefloor, Rachel Johnson entered the Big Brother house, and her father Stanley the I'm a Celebrity jungle, so while celebrities started playing politics, politicians were seemingly behaving more and more like celebrities

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: With the the help of guests including Miles Jupp and Sophie Duker, the comedian tries to make sense of the bewildering year that was 2020, using a combination of stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction to dissect the past 12 months' news

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools Holland takes a look back at some of the many artists that have joined him and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra on that very special night of nights to see in each new year. Featuring performances by global stars like Adele and Michael Bublé to soul, reggae and jazz legends such as Desmond Dekker, Betty Wright and Gregory Porter. Plus, classic duets from the likes of Amy Winehouse and Paul Weller, and Ronnie Wood and Slash

programme.title: Secrets of the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedians who have appeared on Live at the Apollo over the show's first 16 years take a seat in the stalls of the famous Hammersmith theatre, and share their memories of the series. New interviews with the comics provide a unique insight into how it feels to take to British TV's most famous stand-up stage, alongside classic archive clips from the show

programme.title: The Nine Lives of Ozzy Osbourne (2020)
programme.desc: Biography tracing the rock musician's life from his childhood in poverty and spell in prison for burglary, to fronting legendary metal group Black Sabbath and subsequent Grammy Award-winning solo career. More recently, Ozzy has become rock's elder statesman and a loveable family figure. Contributors include wife Sharon Osbourne, son and daughter Jack and Kelly Osbourne, record producer and co-president of Colombia Records Rick Rubin, rappers Ice-T and Poste Malone, as well as Ozzy himself

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC friends ride underwater scooters in Indonesia, try throat singing in Mongolia and pay tribute to the musical Grease while admiring Dong architecture in China

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield and Mark Wright build an 'eco den' for 11-year-old Whitney, an avid recycler who takes a bin bag everywhere she goes so she can deal with any litter she finds

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Brothers Omari and Laquarn make a banana shake breakfast shaky shake shake. This delicious breakfast is the perfect start for a busy day

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari makes plum blackberry crumble as a surprise for his mum

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: A look back at 2020 on Blue Peter, including Mwaksy and Adam joining the show, Lindsey's Train Your Way to Tokyo challenge, and Richie and Adam's mountain karting head-to-head

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Steve Backshall turns detective to solve CBBC viewers' animal mysteries

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: From spiders to ants, wildlife expert Steve Backshall explores the smallest deadly animals

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster joins a team of historians and geologists to recreate a pioneering 280-mile journey through the natural wonder that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Following in Powell's footsteps in the stunning yet challenging north Arizona landscape, Snow and the crew take on the area's rapids in antique wooden boats as they learn about the region's geological and Native American past

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The dung beetle, an insect revered by ancient Egyptians, uses the sun, the moon and even the Milky Way to move its prized ball of dung in the right direction. Pigeons are often considered feeble birdbrains, but they have incredible memories that can recall several complex travel routes with amazing accuracy and they even use man-made roads and hedgerows to find the quickest way home. Here, the veteran broadcaster profiles animals with extraordinary abilities for finding the way

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The second of two festive shows sees The Vivienne, Lesley Joseph, Sabrina Grant and Sally Phillips competing to show off their dress-making skills. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a child's party skirt and then turn old clothes into a recognisable pantomime character in the transformation challenge. They then create a made-to-measure party dress that evokes memories of a particular New Year's Eve - with surprising results

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix for a Christmas treat in his favourite British city, Bristol. First up is a French bistro where they enjoy a stunning cote de boeuf and Michel makes aligot, known as the best mashed potato in the world. The duo then enjoy a swim in one of Britain's oldest lidos, which has an award-winning restaurant serving up a delicious breakfast. Escaping to the countryside, they visit a countryside hotel and restaurant when Fred makes buffalo mozzarella while Michel samples the hotel's own cider, before a return to the city and the multicultural neighbourhood of St Pauls, where they learn to cook Caribbean jerk turkey

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and Paul Ainsworth recreate a dish from the most lavish royal banquet, Dr Polly Russell hears about a bankrupting night out and Michael Caines cooks an Ascot treat

programme.title: Into the Woods (2014)
programme.desc: A wicked witch agrees to lift a curse on a young couple if they will help restore her once beautiful looks. Musical fantasy, based on Stephen Sondheim's hit stage show, which entwines the fairy tale stories of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, Jack and the Beanstalk and Rapunzel. Starring Meryl Streep, James Corden and Emily Blunt, with a cameo by Johnny Depp as the Big Bad Wolf

programme.title: Ice Station Zebra (1968)
programme.desc: An American atomic submarine commander races against time to reach a Soviet spy satellite that has crash-landed near a British research station in the Arctic - before Russian troops arrive to stake their claim to it. Adventure based on Alistair MacLean's novel, starring Rock Hudson, Ernest Borgnine, Jim Brown, Tony Bill and Patrick McGoohan

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the biggest cider factory in the world, which produces more than 350 million litres each year. Cherry Healey explores how the factory is creating new orchards of a sweet apple variety called Scrumptious. And historian Ruth Goodman traces a Victorian apple breeding boom with Dr Annie Gray, recreating one of Queen Victoria's favourite baked apple desserts

programme.title: John Le Mesurier: It's All Been Rather Lovely
programme.desc: A portrait of the prolific actor's life and career, from his turbulent marriage to Hattie Jacques to his renowned role as Sgt Wilson in the TV sitcom Dad's Army and parts in more than 100 films. Featuring contributions by Michael Palin, Clive Dunn, Ian Lavender, Jimmy Perry and Joan Le Mesurier. Narrated by Julian Rhind-Tutt

programme.title: Dad's Army (1971)
programme.desc: The ragtag soldiers of the Walmington-on-Sea Home Guard are given the chance to prove their mettle by apprehending three German aviators who have parachuted into the country in the vanguard of a suspected Nazi invasion. British comedy based on the TV series, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier, Clive Dunn, Bill Pertwee, Ian Lavender and Arnold Ridley

programme.title: Amazing Grace (2018)
programme.desc: Live concert of Aretha Franklin as she recorded her 1972 gospel album Amazing Grace at the New Temple Missionary Baptist Church in Los Angeles. Directed by Sydney Pollack, the film remained unreleased at the time due to technical problems and only saw the light of day in 2018, after the singer's death

programme.title: Aretha Franklin: Respect
programme.desc: A tribute to the singer known as the Queen of Soul, featuring contributions from Tom Jones, Beverley Knight, Clarke Peters and Trevor Nelson among others. Aretha was raised in Detroit where her father preached, and grew up singing gospel. Her career ignited when she signed to Atlantic in 1967 and global hits such as I Say a Little Prayer and Respect quickly established her in the soul genre, while her delivery and regal presence made her an iconic figure in the emerging Civil Rights movement. She renewed success in the 1980s when collaborating with Luther Vandross, cameoing in The Blues Brothers and then duetting with the likes of Annie Lennox and George Michael

programme.title: Entebbe (2018)
programme.desc: Fact-based thriller inspired by the 1976 hijacking of a plane by terrorists at Uganda's Entebbe airport, chronicling negotiations for the release of the hostages on board and their ultimate decision to send in a crack force of commandos. Starring Daniel Bruhl, Rosamunde Pike and Eddie Marsan

programme.title: The Fits (2015)
programme.desc: An 11-year-old tomboy becomes entranced with a tight-knit dance team in Cincinnati's West End. Enamoured by the power and confidence of this strong community of girls, she eagerly absorbs routines, masters drills and even pierces her own ears to fit in. A mysterious outbreak of fainting spells plagues the team, and Toni finds herself caught up in danger as her desire for acceptance is twisted. Teen drama, starring Royalty Hightower and Alexis Neblett

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter V, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets Simon King in Bristol to get a crash course in how to photograph wildlife. They attempt to capture on camera birds in flight over the Avon Gorge, inquisitive city squirrels, and a herd of deer who live semi-wild in the nearby Ashton Court Estate. Plus, a look through Countryfile's archive for some of Ellie's wildlife highlights

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Sussex in a hot-air balloon. She drops in at the gardens of West Dean, where she witnesses the fruits of a 20-year labour of love, and marvels at the transformation of a former car park into an Italian landscape at Arundel Castle. Plus, the contribution the Romans made to British gardening

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In September and October, the horticulturist harvests seeds and pricks out young plants to aid growth. She also prepares the garden for visitors to her Open Garden Scheme day, and enlists her daughters' help to decide the future of a cankered apple tree

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don continues his horticultural tour with a visit to the dreamy climes of Australia and New Zealand. Visiting the renowned Botany Bay, Monty investigates the effect imported plant-life, favoured by colonial settlers, has had on the natural beauty of New South Wales. In New Zealand, the green-fingered presenter goes in search of the wild heart of the country, revelling in a lush rainforest conservatory made entirely of native plants

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer makes mouth-watering chocolate cookies and crispy duck with honey and figs, which prove a little bit of what you fancy does you good

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a history of Westerns, featuring archive interviews with stars including Gary Cooper, James Stewart, Yul Brynner, Paul Newman, John Wayne and Clint Eastwood

programme.title: How the West Was Won (1962)
programme.desc: Three generations of a pioneering family experience many ordeals and adventures in their endeavour to build a new life for themselves on the American frontier in the 19th century. Over the years, the family experiences the key events of the nation's history, including the gold rush, the Civil War, and the building of the railroads. Epic Western, starring James Stewart, John Wayne, Gregory Peck, Henry Fonda, Debbie Reynolds, Lee J Cobb and Richard Widmark

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history and geography of America's deserts, canyons and wilderness, exploring the lives of the people and animals that have found ways to thrive and prosper in these brutally inhospitable conditions. The first programme examines the agricultural traditions of the Hopi Native American tribe in northeastern Arizona, as well as exploring the strategies used by a variety of wildlife, including mustangs, coyotes and desert tortoises, to survive in these demanding environments

programme.title: The Culture Show
programme.desc: Saving Mr Banks charts the troubled production of 1964 children's classic Mary Poppins, which was based on the series of books by PL Travers. In this programme, Victoria Coren Mitchell explores the dark and complex life of the writer, revealing her 20-year battle with Walt Disney and how the big-screen adaptation overshadowed her writings but made her rich. With contributions by Emma Thompson and Cameron Mackintosh

programme.title: Saving Mr Banks (2013)
programme.desc: Walt Disney becomes determined to adapt Mary Poppins into a film, but faces an obstacle in the shape of author PL Travers, who resists the idea of a sentimental Hollywood version of her book. As the movie mogul's charm offensive comes unstuck, he begins to realise why the story is so important to its creator. Fact-based drama, with Emma Thompson, Tom Hanks, Paul Giamatti and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Documentary about the Cornish fishing village of Mevagissey. While many fishing communities are struggling, this one has thrived owing to protection from prevailing winds and returning fish stocks. Boats have been handed down through generations and currently 74 operate out of the harbour. As young captains prepare to follow in their father's footsteps, tourism also becomes a major boost to the town - but leaves it feeling deserted in winter. A veteran skipper prepares to retire, and questions what the next generation will learn from his example

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of ideas for flour-free bread and hair extension storage, as well as an online business proposition from would-be entrepreneurs in the US. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian's world tour of unusual jobs takes her to Georgia, a country bordered by Russia and the Black Sea that has gone through a drastic period of modernisation in the last 10 years - leaving behind some very rare trades with centuries of history. Among the last practitioners of distinctive Georgian skills are a gunsmith, a polyphonic singer and a guide at the Stalin Museum

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: English teacher Claire Wilson arrives for her first term at Westerbrook High full of enthusiasm. Dissatisfied in her marriage, she finds herself attracted to one of her students, and considers an inappropriate relationship that could have crushing consequences for them both. Drama, starring Kate Mara

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: When a party at Logan's house is broken up by the cops, Eric asks for a favour from Claire, who agrees to help, privately knowing that sharing this secret will bring the two of them closer together. Claire struggles to be honest with herself about it, but she is moving Eric closer to crossing a boundary she knows they should not cross

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: The Oxford-born actress, who became an Australian citizen six years ago, embarks on a 10,000km drive around the country discovering more about the place she can now call home. Starting in Bondi, she meets up with an estate agent who reveals the prosperous migrant market. Heading west, Miriam is disturbed to discover that there is a growing number of women who can't afford a home in retirement, now making up the fastest growing number of homeless in the country. With most of eastern Australia in the grip of severe drought, Miriam heads to the country town of Trundle, to see the repercussions for herself

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: After a pre-emptive strike against Hiro and Ando in Africa, Arthur orders Knox and Flint to hunt down his son Nathan and grand-daughter Claire. At Pinehearst, Sylar has a charged confrontation with Elle, while Suresh begins testing his new, combined super-power formula. With the others dead, injured or on the run, Matt tries to wake Angela

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Part one of two. After reuniting Sylar and Elle, Arthur orders the pair to bring Claire to him. Matt, Hiro and Ando follow Daphne to her home in Georgia to discover what she has been hiding. As an eclipse plays havoc with everyone's powers, Peter and Nathan travel to Haiti in search of the only man capable of stopping their father from destroying the world - the Haitian. Guest starring Seth Green (Family Guy)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is locked away in a high-security alien prison. Isolated, alone, with no hope of escape. Far away, on Earth, her best friends, Yaz, Ryan and Graham, have to pick up their lives without her. However, it is not easy as old habits die hard - especially when they discover a disturbing plan forming. A plan that involves a Dalek. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh and John Barrowman

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix for a Christmas treat in his favourite British city, Bristol. First up is a French bistro where they enjoy a stunning cote de boeuf and Michel makes aligot, known as the best mashed potato in the world. The duo then enjoy a swim in one of Britain's oldest lidos, which has an award-winning restaurant serving up a delicious breakfast. Escaping to the countryside, they visit a countryside hotel and restaurant when Fred makes buffalo mozzarella while Michel samples the hotel's own cider, before a return to the city and the multicultural neighbourhood of St Pauls, where they learn to cook Caribbean jerk turkey

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: New Year's Day Concert
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny introduces highlights of the New Year's Day Concert from the Musikverein in Vienna. Riccardo Muti conducts the Vienna Philharmonic in a traditional feast of music by the Strauss family and their contemporaries

programme.title: Whitney at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of songs by one of soul music's biggest-ever voices, illustrating why the world sat up and listened when Whitney Houston burst onto the scene in 1985 with Saving All My Love for You. This playlist traverses the '80s, '90s and '00s, leading to the star's early death in 2012, which was wildly regarded as a tragic loss to the world of contemporary music

programme.title: Whitney: Can I Be Me (2017)
programme.desc: Director Nick Broomfield makes use of previously unused footage and live performance recordings to tell the life story of Whitney Houston, the celebrated American singer who died in 2012 aged 48. Despite making millions of dollars, and achieving more consecutive number-one hits than The Beatles, Whitney's time in the spotlight left her believing she 'wasn't free' to be herself, and here, Broomfield assesses the circumstances that would bring a talented performer of international repute to believe she was a victim of her own success

programme.title: Queens of Soul
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage to celebrate the critically-acclaimed female singers whose voices have come to define the soul genre, including Aretha Franklin, Roberta Flack, Gladys Knight, Randy Crawford, Angie Stone, Mary J Blige and Beyonce

programme.title: Lionel Richie at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by the singer, including his first Top of the Pops appearance with the Commodores in 1979 and highlights from his 2009 concert at the BBC's Maida Vale studios

programme.title: How Auld Lang Syne Took Over the World
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond examines how Robert Burns' 18th-century poem, traditionally sung around the world on New Year's Eve, became one of Scotland's best-known musical exports. From Ayrshire to Tokyo, via New York City, he finds out how Auld Lang Syne has been adopted by different cultures, with commentary from well-known faces including Alan Cumming, Cliff Richard and Clare Grogan

programme.title: Bruce Dickinson: Scream for Me Sarajevo (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about a 1994 concert by Bruce Dickinson and his band Skunkworks in Sarajevo, when the capital of Bosnia and Herzegovina was in its third year under siege during the ongoing Bosnian War. Featuring personal accounts of the band members who risked their lives to perform in a war zone, and the fans who attended the concert while living in the midst of continual violence

programme.title: Simon King's Shetland Diaries
programme.desc: The wildlife cameraman sets out on an adventure with his wife Marguerite Smits Van Oyen and daughter Savannah to experience life on the Shetland Islands for a year. Arriving in the summer of 2008, Simon searches for an orca whale, sets up a hide to observe Arctic terns and teaches his daughter how to spot gannets. As winter arrives, the weather begins to cause problems when they are marooned by high winds and tides

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor begins his trek across the Sahara Desert. Landing in Morocco after crossing the Straits of Gibraltar, he proceeds to scale the High Atlas mountains and meets the Sahawari people, who provide welcome assistance in an inhospitable land. He also rides on the world's longest train, and plays a game of desert draughts

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: With Gilou facing the possibility of prison, Laure's team try to recover their reputation by taking on a case that no-one else wants - the murder of an identified minor in a run-down part of town. The return of the French detective drama, starring Caroline Proust

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Gilou makes headway as an informer and gets close to Cisco, a dangerous convict coming to the end of his sentence who is being monitored by the police. Josephine Karlsson meets Souleymane, the 13-year-old Moroccan boy accused of the murder Laure and Ali are investigating. In French

programme.title: Classic Soul at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances from the BBC archive by acclaimed soul singers, including Aretha Franklin, Al Green, Isaac Hayes, Dusty Springfield, Solomon Burke and Percy Sledge

programme.title: Whitney at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of songs by one of soul music's biggest-ever voices, illustrating why the world sat up and listened when Whitney Houston burst onto the scene in 1985 with Saving All My Love for You. This playlist traverses the '80s, '90s and '00s, leading to the star's early death in 2012, which was wildly regarded as a tragic loss to the world of contemporary music

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools Holland takes a look back at some of the many artists that have joined him and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra on that very special night of nights to see in each new year. Featuring performances by global stars like Adele and Michael Bublé to soul, reggae and jazz legends such as Desmond Dekker, Betty Wright and Gregory Porter. Plus, classic duets from the likes of Amy Winehouse and Paul Weller, and Ronnie Wood and Slash

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor begins his trek across the Sahara Desert. Landing in Morocco after crossing the Straits of Gibraltar, he proceeds to scale the High Atlas mountains and meets the Sahawari people, who provide welcome assistance in an inhospitable land. He also rides on the world's longest train, and plays a game of desert draughts

programme.title: Simon King's Shetland Diaries
programme.desc: The wildlife cameraman sets out on an adventure with his wife Marguerite Smits Van Oyen and daughter Savannah to experience life on the Shetland Islands for a year. Arriving in the summer of 2008, Simon searches for an orca whale, sets up a hide to observe Arctic terns and teaches his daughter how to spot gannets. As winter arrives, the weather begins to cause problems when they are marooned by high winds and tides

programme.title: Aled Jones at 50
programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his remarkable musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano. Throughout the programme, Aled discovers archive of himself as a boy he had no idea existed, and also reflects on some of the highlights of his career, singing for Pope John Paul, the Royal family and at the wedding of Bob Geldof and Paula Yates

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic police's anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques

programme.title: Joanna Lumley in the Land of the Northern Lights
programme.desc: The actress travels up through Norway to Svalbard, the most northerly permanently inhabited place on Earth, where she experiences temperatures approaching minus 30C. Her aim is to fulfil a lifetime ambition to see the natural phenomenon, the Northern Lights. Along the way she explores the fjords of Lofoten, learns to ride a snowmobile and spends a night in a hotel made of ice

programme.title: Max Richter's Sleep (2019)
programme.desc: Cameras follow acclaimed composer and musician Max Richter and his creative partner, artist and film-maker Yulia Mahr, as they navigate an ambitious performance of his eight-hour opus Sleep at an open-air concert in Los Angeles

programme.title: Upstream
programme.desc: Over three years, cameras follow the course of the river Dee in Scotland to its source in the Cairngorm mountains, the highest of any river in Britain. The film, by writer Robert Macfarlane and director Rob Petit, begins in the lowland floodplains of the Dee, before travelling up, against the flow of the river, into Britain's own Arctic wilderness

programme.title: Aled Jones at 50
programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his remarkable musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano.
