programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A half-asleep Love Monster exits his house and walks towards the green, passing snoozing citizens on the way. He notices that the whole of the green is covered with litter and snoring Fluffytowners. Love Monster remembers that yesterday was Woo Hoo Day and the whole of Fluffytown had a big party - which makes today the Day After Woo Hoo Day. Love Monster attempts to clean up all of the mess on his own as a nice surprise for his friends, but he struggles and soon realises that it is a very big job. He should ask for help! He wakes everyone up, saying if everyone picks up one piece of litter each, they can tidy up the green in no time. Elder Kitten says he knows just the song for the job. Love Monster and Elder Kitten lead the Fluffytowners in singing a funky, tidying up song as they each pick up a piece of litter to clean the green. Everyone takes their piece of litter to the recycling centre where they queue up to do their bit for the environment.

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: In this episode, we follow Annabelle and her family as they celebrate bringing in the New Year. After carefully decorating her New Year's Eve invitations, Annabelle sets off delivering them to her friends so that they can join in the party. With a little help from her dad, it's time to prepare for the guests' arrival. When everyone arrives, that's when the fun really starts. After dressing up and taking some photos together, the children play a competitive game of limbo followed by a hilarious game of musical statues. But they need to hurry and finish because it's almost midnight! After counting down the seconds to bring in the New Year, the celebrations begin. There's one last thing Annabelle still has to do: watch their own spectacular fireworks display! The next morning, it's New Year's Day, so Annabelle and her family decide to make a time capsule to help them reflect on the previous year and write down their New Year's resolutions.

programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

Unable fit inside, Glitch uses his size-ray to shrink himself and zooms after the ant. Inside the tree, the ants see Glitch as a threat and start to surround him. programme.desc: Charlie Stayt presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Captain Underpants: The First Epic Movie (2017)
programme.desc: George and Harold, a pair of elementary school pranksters, inadvertently transform their mean-spirited principal into a daring vigilante. Cartoon adventure based on the best-selling series of books, featuring the voices of Kevin Hart, Thomas Middleditch and Ed Helms

programme.desc: The grouchy ogre celebrates Halloween, his favourite holiday, by challenging Fiona, Donkey, Puss in Boots and the other fairy-tale characters to spend the night in Lord Farquaad's haunted castle telling scary stories. The last one to be scared 'Shrekless' will be declared the winner. Animation based on the film series, featuring the voices of Mike Myers and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: The Boss Baby (2017)
programme.desc: A youngster believes his baby brother is actually a power-hungry tycoon sent to destroy his perfect family life. However, the duo must put aside their differences and work together when they uncover a plot to sabotage the company their parents work for. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Alec Baldwin, Steve Buscemi, Jimmy Kimmel and Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's second children's book about the accident-prone dragon. Zog is helping his friend Princess Pearl to provide much-needed medical care to all manner of creatures, assisted by knight Sir Gadabout. However, when a storm forces them to return to Pearl's home at the palace, her uncle the king does not approve of her career - at least until he falls ill himself. Narrated by Lenny Henry, with the voices of Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Archbishop of Canterbury's New Year Message

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.desc: A young meerkat queen lives in the harsh beauty of the Madgadikgadi salt pans. If her new pups are to survive, she must unite her family in the face of rivals, predators and immense dust storms. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World (2019)
programme.desc: Viking warrior Hiccup and his faithful dragon Toothless venture out into a hidden world to protect their village from the darkest threat yet. Third chapter in the animated fantasy saga, featuring the voices of Jay Baruchel, America Ferrera and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming (2019)
programme.desc: A decade after events of How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World, brave warrior Hiccup and faithful sidekick Toothless reunite. They attempt to remind both their kinds of the inseparable bond between Vikings and dragons as they prepare for the Snoggletog Festival. Animated drama, with the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves (1991)
programme.desc: English nobleman Robin of Locksley returns home from fighting in the Crusades, only to discover that his father has been murdered and his lands seized, while the local people are living in fear under the tyrannical rule of the Sheriff of Nottingham. Branded an outlaw for his defiance, he and his Moorish travelling companion go into hiding in the forest, where they recruit a group of bandits to fight for justice, protect the downtrodden, and foil the Sheriff's plan to seize the throne while the king is away at war. Medieval action adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Alan Rickman, Morgan Freeman, Mary Elizabeth Mastrantonio, Christian Slater and Mike McShane

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is locked away in a high-security alien prison. Isolated, alone, with no hope of escape. Far away, on Earth, her best friends, Yaz, Ryan and Graham, have to pick up their lives without her. However, it is not easy as old habits die hard - especially when they discover a disturbing plan forming. A plan that involves a Dalek. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh and John Barrowman

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda plans for her future, while Mick prepares to confront his deepest fears head on - but when the moment arrives, will he be able to go through with it? Stacey, Kat and Martin are worried about Kush, Stuart finds himself in trouble, and Jay snaps at Ben over comments about his love life. Patrick goes through with a deal, but it may prove costly

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Eight-part drama inspired by real events, telling the remarkable story of the capture of the French fraudster and killer, Charles Sobhraj. When a junior diplomat at the Dutch Embassy in Bangkok unwittingly walks into Charles Sobhraj's intricate web of crime, he sets off an extraordinary chain of events as he attempts to bring Sobhraj to justice for his crimes. Starring Tahar Rahim, Jenna Coleman, Billy Howle and Ellie Bamber

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod is thrown in at the deep ends as he joins the rigorous Royal Navy training scheme - which isn't good news for a man who struggles with seasickness. He is also given a chance to take the helm of one of Her Majesty's warships - only to take this crash-course in marine navigation all too literally

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel

programme.title: Bad Education
programme.desc: Mr Fraser appoints Alfie as director for the Christmas production, and decides to cast the school bully as the lead in a combination of Robocop and the Nutcracker, called Robocracker. Meanwhile, Mitchell tries to lure Alfie's estranged mother from her home in Spain as a surprise, and Miss Gulliver attempts to persuade her class to volunteer in a soup kitchen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live

Oliver's family, from Appleton in Cheshire, has budgeted £3,000 for the budget, and it's going to need every penny of that to successfully transform the present bland suburban space into a cross between a race track for Oliver and a show garden for parents Sally and Ian

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Chelsea v Manchester City and Southampton v Liverpool in the Premier League

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets a photographer attempting to capture images of the UK's remaining art deco cinemas

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple who hope to realise their dream of escaping to Devon. They have a generous budget of £1.4million, and hope to find a property with land, a self-contained annex and lots of room for guests. Away from country homes, Jules visits a sustainable family farm producing food in harmony with nature

programme.title: Big Hero 6 (2014)
programme.desc: A lonely, orphaned computer genius forms a special bond with an inflatable robot. When a devastating event occurs in their futuristic home city, they join forces with a group of friends to form a hi-tech band of heroes who are determined to get to the bottom of the matter. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Ryan Potter, Scott Adsit and Daniel Henney

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team revamp a pair of brown atomic chairs with the help of upholsterer Anthony Devine, and later find a mid-century hall unit that needs bringing up to date

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen is on a quest for cash, as he reviews the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of one-armed bandits, potato rustlers and £30 pints, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Poet Lemn Sissay, BBC Radio 1 DJ Arielle Free, actress Crissy Rock and sports broadcaster Andrew Cotter go under the spotlight tonight answering questions on Sylvia Pankhurst, Daft Punk, The Pendle Witch Trials and Smallfilms

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous names from radio take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Johnnie Walker, Sally Boazman, Stuart Maconie, Elizabeth Alker, Scott Mills, Chris Stark, Vick Hope and Harriet Scott

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Connie struggles to protect her team when the coronavirus pandemic hits Holby ED, Will becomes disillusioned with his work and Fenisha rushes to keep her baby safe

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy's sister, who has a reputation for wild living, arrives in time to throw the glamorous socialite's New Year party plans into disarray. Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley star, with guest Kate O'Mara

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Leeds United. The north London rivals involved in these fixtures have enjoyed contrasting fortunes this season, with Spurs near the top of the table, and Arsenal floundering in the lower reaches following a series of home defeats

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of the latest matches and look ahead to the concluding regular-season fixtures

programme.title: Internal Affairs (1990)
programme.desc: A crooked cop uses his badge and uniform as a cover for his unsavoury money-making schemes and is prepared to murder anyone who stands in his way. A zealous investigator vows to bring him to justice, but the cunning detective responds by turning his adversary's wife against him. Crime thriller, starring Richard Gere, Andy Garcia, Laurie Metcalf, Nancy Travis and William Baldwin

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Leeds United. The north London rivals involved in these fixtures have enjoyed contrasting fortunes this season, with Spurs near the top of the table, and Arsenal floundering in the lower reaches following a series of home defeats

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Henry Hull leads the ecumenical service for the Second Sunday of Christmas from Down Cathedral in Downpatrick, Co Down. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a large two-bedroom apartment in Battersea, south-west London, while Martin Roberts looks over plans to develop a house in Winsford, Cheshire

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross oversees oversees proceedings at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells as two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell on for profit. Experts Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant attempt to guide the sides to success at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores St Paul's Cathedral, with music including hymns sung by the Cathedral Choir and performances by Aled Jones and the Priests, and Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: Ranger Grace finds an orphaned 10-year-old boy in the forest, where he has been ever since the death of his parents five years earlier. What's more, he tells her he has been living with a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Could her father's tales of a mythical beast be true after all? Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway goes on a walk from his native Barry along the coast road to Rhoose Point. Later, he heads north for a trek among the foothills of Snowdonia's Carneddau mountains, along the Huw Tom Trail

programme.title: Aled Jones at 50
programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano. Throughout the programme, Aled discovers archive of himself as a boy he had no idea existed, and also reflects on some of the highlights of his career, singing for Pope John Paul, the Royal family and at the wedding of Bob Geldof and Paula Yates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Captain Underpants: The First Epic Movie (2017)
programme.desc: George and Harold, a pair of elementary school pranksters, inadvertently transform their mean-spirited principal into a daring vigilante. Cartoon adventure based on the best-selling series of books, featuring the voices of Kevin Hart, Thomas Middleditch and Ed Helms

programme.title: Scared Shrekless
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre celebrates Halloween, his favourite holiday, by challenging Fiona, Donkey, Puss in Boots and the other fairy-tale characters to spend the night in Lord Farquaad's haunted castle telling scary stories. The last one to be scared 'Shrekless' will be declared the winner. Animation based on the film series, featuring the voices of Mike Myers and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: The Boss Baby (2017)
programme.desc: A youngster believes his baby brother is actually a power-hungry tycoon sent to destroy his perfect family life. However, the duo must put aside their differences and work together when they uncover a plot to sabotage the company their parents work for. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Alec Baldwin, Steve Buscemi, Jimmy Kimmel and Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's second children's book about the accident-prone dragon. Zog is helping his friend Princess Pearl to provide much-needed medical care to all manner of creatures, assisted by knight Sir Gadabout. However, when a storm forces them to return to Pearl's home at the palace, her uncle the king does not approve of her career - at least until he falls ill himself. Narrated by Lenny Henry, with the voices of Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Resolutions
programme.desc: The Rt Rev Martin Fair, Moderator of the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland, looks back on 2020 through the work of Peter and Heidi Gardner who are making a six-foot wreath made of rosemary. Sprigs of the herb have been sent to them from all over Scotland as symbols of the sadness and loss people have experienced during this year of the pandemic. Their project, Scented Lament, represents the notion of looking forward rather than backward

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: A young meerkat queen lives in the harsh beauty of the Madgadikgadi salt pans. If her new pups are to survive, she must unite her family in the face of rivals, predators and immense dust storms. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World (2019)
programme.desc: Viking warrior Hiccup and his faithful dragon Toothless venture out into a hidden world to protect their village from the darkest threat yet. Third chapter in the animated fantasy saga, featuring the voices of Jay Baruchel, America Ferrera and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming (2019)
programme.desc: A decade after events of How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World, brave warrior Hiccup and faithful sidekick Toothless reunite. They attempt to remind both their kinds of the inseparable bond between Vikings and dragons as they prepare for the Snoggletog Festival. Animated drama, with the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves (1991)
programme.desc: English nobleman Robin of Locksley returns home from fighting in the Crusades, only to discover that his father has been murdered and his lands seized, while the local people are living in fear under the tyrannical rule of the Sheriff of Nottingham. Branded an outlaw for his defiance, he and his Moorish travelling companion go into hiding in the forest, where they recruit a group of bandits to fight for justice, protect the downtrodden, and foil the Sheriff's plan to seize the throne while the king is away at war. Medieval action adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Alan Rickman, Morgan Freeman, Mary Elizabeth Mastrantonio, Christian Slater and Mike McShane

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor is locked away in a high-security alien prison. Isolated, alone, with no hope of escape. Far away, on Earth, her best friends, Yaz, Ryan and Graham, have to pick up their lives without her. However, it is not easy as old habits die hard - especially when they discover a disturbing plan forming. A plan that involves a Dalek. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jodie Whittaker, Bradley Walsh and John Barrowman

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda plans for her future, while Mick prepares to confront his deepest fears head on - but when the moment arrives, will he be able to go through with it? Stacey, Kat and Martin are worried about Kush, Stuart finds himself in trouble, and Jay snaps at Ben over comments about his love life. Patrick goes through with a deal, but it may prove costly

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Eight-part drama inspired by real events, telling the remarkable story of the capture of the French fraudster and killer, Charles Sobhraj. When a junior diplomat at the Dutch Embassy in Bangkok unwittingly walks into Charles Sobhraj's intricate web of crime, he sets off an extraordinary chain of events as he attempts to bring Sobhraj to justice for his crimes. Starring Tahar Rahim, Jenna Coleman, Billy Howle and Ellie Bamber

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy plan some New Year's Eve festivities in this Christmas special, with both inviting their parents to join them. Things soon go badly wrong as party games give way to an uncompromising round of guests pointing out what New Year's resolutions everyone needs to make - with a distinct lack of festive spirit on display. Starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Nina Warhurst present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ching He-Huang and Ben Tish, and special guest Grace Dent. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich compete to create a garden incorporating a BMX track for youngster Ollie, who has yet to reach the age of 10. Oliver's family, from Appleton in Cheshire, has budgeted £3,000 for the budget, and it's going to need every penny of that to successfully transform the present bland suburban space into a cross between a race track for Oliver and a show garden for parents Sally and Ian

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Chelsea v Manchester City and Southampton v Liverpool in the Premier League

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern antiques and collectors fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles meets a photographer attempting to capture images of the UK's remaining art deco cinemas

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson joins a couple who hope to realise their dream of escaping to Devon. They have a generous budget of £1.4million, and hope to find a property with land, a self-contained annex and lots of room for guests. Away from country homes, Jules visits a sustainable family farm producing food in harmony with nature

programme.title: Big Hero 6 (2014)
programme.desc: A lonely, orphaned computer genius forms a special bond with an inflatable robot. When a devastating event occurs in their futuristic home city, they join forces with a group of friends to form a hi-tech band of heroes who are determined to get to the bottom of the matter. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Ryan Potter, Scott Adsit and Daniel Henney

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where items saved from the skip include a wooden clothes airer and a modern fire surround

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. Poet Lemn Sissay, BBC Radio 1 DJ Arielle Free, actress Crissy Rock and sports broadcaster Andrew Cotter go under the spotlight tonight answering questions on Sylvia Pankhurst, Daft Punk, The Pendle Witch Trials and Smallfilms

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous names from radio take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Johnnie Walker, Sally Boazman, Stuart Maconie, Elizabeth Alker, Scott Mills, Chris Stark, Vick Hope and Harriet Scott

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Connie struggles to protect her team when the coronavirus pandemic hits Holby ED, Will becomes disillusioned with his work and Fenisha rushes to keep her baby safe

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Helping them this time round are Stacey Dooley, Roman Kemp, Omid Djalili, Judy Murray, Owain Wyn Evans, Kate Humble and Vikkstar

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy's sister, who has a reputation for wild living, arrives in time to throw the glamorous socialite's New Year party plans into disarray. Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley star, with guest Kate O'Mara

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures, as the Old Firm derby between Rangers and Celtic at Ibrox took centre-stage. Plus, Aberdeen v Dundee United, Hamilton Academical v Motherwell, Hibernian v Livingston, Kilmarnock v St Mirren and Ross County v St Johnstone

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Bromwich Albion v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Leeds United. The north London rivals involved in these fixtures have enjoyed contrasting fortunes this season, with Spurs near the top of the table, and Arsenal floundering in the lower reaches following a series of home defeats

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of the latest matches and look ahead to the concluding regular-season fixtures

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Very Rev Henry Hull leads the ecumenical service for the Second Sunday of Christmas from Down Cathedral in Downpatrick, Co Down. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: The Rev Jane Howitt of St Rollox and Fr Dermot Preston of St Aloysius, both Glasgow, present from the foyer of BBC Glasgow, with live music by Rachel Hair and Ron Jappy.

programme.title: Planes: Fire & Rescue (2014)
programme.desc: A racing plane suffers damage to his engine, and realises he can never compete again. He starts a new career as a member of a firefighting squadron, which proves even more dangerous than he expected when the aircraft have to deal with a massive forest blaze. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Dane Cook and Ed Harris

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross oversees oversees proceedings at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells as two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell on for profit. Experts Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant attempt to guide the sides to success at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins explores St Paul's Cathedral, with music including hymns sung by the Cathedral Choir and performances by Aled Jones and the Priests, and Daniel O'Donnell

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: Ranger Grace finds an orphaned 10-year-old boy in the forest, where he has been ever since the death of his parents five years earlier. What's more, he tells her he has been living with a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Could her father's tales of a mythical beast be true after all? Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Leamington Spa to help Robert and Gemma and their daughter Darcey, who is afraid of the family's nettle-strewn, overgrown garden

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Pete's Dragon (2016)
programme.desc: Ranger Grace finds an orphaned 10-year-old boy in the forest, where he has been ever since the death of his parents five years earlier. What's more, he tells her he has been living with a giant, fire-breathing dragon. Could her father's tales of a mythical beast be true after all? Fantasy adventure, starring Bryce Dallas Howard, Robert Redford and Oakes Fegley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Leamington Spa to help Robert and Gemma and their daughter Darcey, who is afraid of the family's nettle-strewn, overgrown garden

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher looks back at 2020, a difficult year when the countryside became a lifeline for unprecedented numbers during the pandemic, while wildlife made their homes in curious places during lockdown. Sean also catches up with young people at the forefront of the battle to protect the environment

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns with Fiona Bruce and the team visiting Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items brought in for assessment include a set of Star Wars toys, and a policeman's helmet worn by John Lennon. There is also a rock crystal Faberge cigarette case complete with a ruby, an early post-box, and a poster for one of Pink Floyd's very first concerts

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary revealing how the forces of nature drive, shape and support the Earth's great diversity of life. The first edition examines volcanoes, which responsible for both for the planet's breathable atmosphere and the oceans, but are also the architects of the planet, with over 80% of the Earth's surface being the result of magma bursting up from the molten interior - providing a platform for life

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Marie-Andree Leclerc falls under the spell of Charles Sobhraj while on holiday in India and is persuaded to move to Bangkok to live with him, but he does not give her the life he promised he. When Charles does takes Marie-Andrée to the beach, he enlists her to befriend an Australian couple, who he drugs and robs of their money and passports. Drama, starring Tahar Rahim and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, featuring Chelsea v Manchester City, Burnley v Fulham and Newcastle United v Leicester City. Neither Chelsea nor Manchester City have found the consistency yet this season to suggest that winning the title is within their grasp, but victory against their rivals here would send out a statement to the rest of the challengers

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The second of two festive specials of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll. Winnie has not been sleeping well, and wants to tell Agnes about her dreams, so when her house is later burgled and her mother's wedding ring stolen, Buster and Dermot plan to take matters into their own hands to expose the thief. Cathy has a significant birthday coming up - and is far from happy about it

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: The statement in the Scottish Parliament by the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon, on the Covid-19 pandemic and the possible need to strengthen the top level of Covid-19 restrictions in Scotland as the whole of the Scottish mainland moves to level 4 from Boxing Day, from Tuesday 22 December

programme.desc: Interviews with newsmakers and personalities from across the globe

programme.title: Briefings

programme.title: Briefings

programme.title: House of Commons

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of the last question session of 2020 in the House of Lords

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on the economics of music streaming with evidence from Colin Young, Tom Gray, Tom Frederikse, Guy Garvey, Ed O'Brien, Nadine Shah, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Lt General HR McMaster who served as Mr Trump's national security adviser until he was fired in 2018. Will the Trump years be seen as an aberration or a marker of change?

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Coverage of a debate on the future of football in Westminster Hall, from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Dr Chris Whitty, the Chief Medical Officer for England, delivering a lecture at Gresham College on 'the Geography of Ill Health', from Wednesday 25 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint session of the Health and Social Care and Science and Technology committees. MPs will hear from Prof Devi Sridhar, Professor of Global Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Health and Social Care Secretary Matt Hancock, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Scientists have discovered water on the sunlit surface of the moon for the first time but does it matter? The moon is back in vogue in terms of space exploration - the US says it will put astronauts back on the lunar surface by 2024. It's supposed to be the precursor to a manned mission to Mars. Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, Chief Exploration Scientist at NASA. In this time of pandemic and climate change here on earth is space exploration a potential lifeline or a massive vanity project?

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Lords on the Integrated Review of Security, Defence, Development and Foreign Policy, from earlier today

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: C-SPAN's coverage of the Senate Judiciary committee hearing on the role of tech giants during the presidential election campaign with Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey, from Tuesday 17 November.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Westminster Hall - Funding for Transport for London Coverage of a debate in Westminster Hall on funding for Transport for London, from Monday 23 November

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions to the Executive Office in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The short debate in the House of Commons marking the 60th anniversary of Coronation Street, opened by the MP and former Coronation Street actor, Tracy Brabin, from Wednesday 2 December

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London, from Wednesday 23 December.

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament
programme.desc: Conservative-led debate in the Welsh Parliament on the impact of Covid on health services, from Wednesday 25 November. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 28 November

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of the day's proceedings in the House of Commons including backbench business debates on the regulation and prevention of online harms and on International Men's Day followed by an adjournment debate on the Department for Health and Social Care's Independent Reconfiguration Panel.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: As soon as he emerged from his coma Alexei Navalny, the Russian opposition leader apparently poisoned by novichok nerve agent, expressed his determination to return to Moscow. But what future is there for an anti-Putin political movement in a country where dissent is all too often seriously bad for your health? Stephen Sackur speaks to Leonid Volkov, opposition politician and chief of staff to Mr Navalny. Is there any weakening of the Kremlin's grip on power?

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Peers quiz government minister Lord Parkinson on plans for an additional bank holiday to mark the Queen's Platinum Jubilee in 2022, from Monday 16 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland Affairs select committee session on cross-border cooperation on policing, security and criminal justice with evidence from the Police Service of Northern Ireland, the National Crime Agency and Her Majesty's Customs and Revenue, from Wednesday 4 November.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the point of the world's nuclear watchdog the International Atomic Energy Agency? Its task is to ensure that countries intent on developing nuclear power don't use their programmes as cover for development of weapons of mass destruction. But is the task impossible? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi. From the continued bitter arguments over Iran, to North Korea, and Saudi Arabia, is the IAEA another example of a global agency undermined by geopolitical division?

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the International Trade Secretary Liz Truss and her team of ministers.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Scientists have discovered water on the sunlit surface of the moon for the first time, but does it matter? The moon is back in vogue in terms of space exploration - the US says it will put astronauts back on the lunar surface by 2024. It's supposed to be the precursor to a manned mission to Mars. Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, chief exploration scientist at Nasa. In this time of pandemic and climate change here on Earth, is space exploration a potential lifeline or a massive vanity project?

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Iorram - Boat Song
programme.desc: A cinema documentary entirely in Scottish Gaelic, Iorram (Boat Song) is a lyrical portrait of life in Scotland's Outer Hebrides, past and present. Director Alastair Cole takes the audience on an immersive journey into the heart and soul of a 1000-year-old community, blending archive sound recordings of voices from the past with visuals of island life today and an original score by acclaimed folk musician Aidan O'Rourke. At the core of the film is an extraordinary trove of sound archive, recorded by pioneering Scottish ethnographers in the mid-20th century, who visited the Western Isles to capture the hardship and romance of life lived in precarious balance with the sea. These newly restored recordings preserve an oral history of lore and legends, tall tales and tragedies, passed down through generations of Gaelic speakers. This soundtrack of voices, stories and songs from the past is accompanied by moving imagery of the daily working rhythm of the islands, on land and on water, shot ov

programme.title: An Sgoil Bheag Mu Dheireadh
programme.desc: The story of a small remote school's fight to survive in June 2013, which would have left six children with a school and a piece of the community broken

programme.title: God Save The Quine
programme.desc: Fiona MacKenzie explores the journeys travelled by Scotland's most revered female musicians - Annie Lennox, Barbara Dickson, Sharleen Spiteri, Eddie Reader, Clare Grogan, Lorrain Mckintosh, KT Tunstall, Emma Pollock, Amy McDonald, Lauren Mayberry, Be Charlotte and newly emerging talent Kitti

programme.title: Mach a Seo: Na Fir-chlis
programme.desc: Since reading about the aurora borealis in his childhood, Ramsay MacMahon has dreamt of seeing this natural phenomenon. But after years of watching and waiting for the elusive Northern Lights to appear, he can wait to longer. He’s set himself a challenge - to see the aurora before the winter is out. Harnessing the power of the technology available to him, he redoubles his efforts to be in the right place at the right time to witness the aurora. Will he succeed, or will these magical lights continue to elude him?

programme.desc: A boy living in a medieval monastery under constant attack by Viking raiders acquires an unfinished book of mystical knowledge. The task of completing the tome falls to him, so he ventures into a haunted forest in search of the secrets required, where he must face his own darkest fears. Animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kathleen MacInnes, David Walker, Catherine Tinney, Iain MacRae and Iain S MacPherson

programme.title: Iron Women/Boireannaich Iarainn
programme.desc: The history of women's golf in Scotland. From the early pioneers of the 18th century to formidable role models who challenged the patriarchal constraints of male-dominated golfing arenas, this story celebrates the trailblazers who put Scottish women's golf firmly on the world map

programme.title: Dystonia: Beatha air Fhiaradh
programme.desc: For almost 40 years, DK MacPhee has lived with dystonia, which leaves him unable to straighten his head. DK gives an insight into the impact this has on his daily life and his ongoing battle to find treatment or relief. As his condition has deteriorated, with his head falling further to one side, the more his life has been affected. A number of years ago, he had to give up playing football, weekly, with his friends, something which he very much misses and he is determined to try and play again. Will this be possible?

programme.title: NY2SY
programme.desc: In a focused and candid delivery, Niall Iain Macdonald recounts his adventure story, attempting to conquer the might of the Atlantic Ocean in a solo rowing boat. Supported by footage taken over several years, both ashore and at sea, he shares the dramatic highs and the extreme lows of his 2018 adventure. In the lead-up to his latest attempt he records his slow, steady progress, the cancellations, postponements, accidents and frustrations that met him along the way, and also the sheer elation of finally setting out to sea. Illustrated with unique footage captured on board as he rowed

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Erik confronts Ingrid over Adam's dark side and is surprised to discover that his brother's life was far from perfect. Karin struggles at school, but when Erik tries to play the role of a dedicated father, he only reinforces her suspicion that something is seriously wrong and she decides to take matters into her own hands. In Norwegian

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: If only Mike were a little bit more muscular, Iris would definitely look at him in a new light. At least that's what he tells himself when he sees Stephan's new workout bench. Unfortunately, it's not that easy

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Louise snaps a photo of Mike while they play a game together. The only problem is that Mike looks absolutely ridiculous in it

programme.title: Mighty Mike

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Pedro decides to try his hand at casting a curse

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis loses a casket with Aunt Lydia resting inside

programme.title: Lego Jurassic World: Double Trouble
programme.desc: Jurassic World is open for business, but with a few changes - a sibling rivalry has led to new management, but things get out of hand quickly when the new boss goes off the rails

programme.title: Lego Jurassic World: Double Trouble
programme.desc: Jurassic World has developed a new species of dinosaur and they're on the loose - will the new dinosaurs be caught before things go wild?

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Walks: Top 100
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Ore Oduba present a countdown of the UK's best loved walks, voted for by more than 8,000 enthusiasts. Featuring locations from the West Highland Way to the Ridgeway, and almost everywhere in between. Famous faces including Catherine Tyldesley, Adrian Edmondson, Katherine Kelly, Larry Lamb, Janet Street Porter and Robert Bathurst hit the trails to reveal their own favourite walks, which feature in the poll. They are joined by experts, nature aficionados and members of the public who provide an insight into the hikes, the history and the hidden stories behind the trails

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of seven races fom Exeter, Musselburgh and Tramore The highlight is the Savills New Year Chase at Tramore, which features the return of dual Gold Cup winner Al Boum Photo in a fascinating contest. There are also competitive handicaps across the card from Exeter and Musselburgh. With analysis from AP McCoy and Luke Harvey, betting news from Rishi Persad, reporting from Alice Plunkett and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray invites three pairs of contestants to call upon their powers of deduction in a battle of words. They must correctly and quickly work out the words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show, potentially doubling their winnings or leaving with nothing. First up are father and son Simon and Will, competition fanatic Sue and husband Paul, and mother and daughter Dawn and Regan

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Oli, Sophie, Cina and Bob pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Harriet struggles, Dawn panics, and it remains to be seen whether Paul will open up to Mandy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Craig's intervention leads to Gary's arrest, Dev angers his neighbours by fraternising with Ray, and Steve shares a fundraising idea in Oliver's memory

programme.title: Dancing on Thin Ice with Torvill & Dean
programme.desc: Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean fulfil a lifelong dream by travelling to Alaska to skate on natural ice, having only ever performed on artificially created rinks in their 45 years dancing together. Travelling by train and husky sled, they encounter first-hand the dramatic effects of climate change on the wilderness. Alaska's hottest year on record means there is not a lot of ice to be found, so Jayne and Chris's trek becomes a hunt, skating wherever they can hoping to find a magnificent natural setting to dance a special new version of their iconic Bolero. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Pitch Perfect (2012)
programme.desc: A new student finds herself a social outcast during her first year of college, but soon acquires a new circle of friends in a band of misfits with a shared talent for singing. She joins their a cappella group and leads them to compete in the campus music competitions. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Rebel Wilson and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh meets his match in former international CIA agent Wesley Cole (Seann William Scott), who returns home and assumes duty as an LAPD officer. Cole soon finds himself navigating a new partnership with Murtaugh as he adjusts to life in Los Angeles, while Avery continues his campaign for city council. With Damon Wayans, Keesha Sharp and Kevin Rahm

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Today's the big day, and Magoo believes he is in a recording studio to make a hit single for famous producer, Phil Dustspeck. But is that really true?

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz tests a ray that makes people dance. Magoo, wearing a snorkelling mask he's convinced is a virtual reality gaming headset, mistakes the ray for a weapon.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he's on an Arctic expedition while actually at the beach, Magoo mistakes Fizz for a monkey, definitive evidence of global warming.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracys leave their island home to visit a family estate, but the appearance of a mysterious intruder threatens to break the peace. Animated adventure, with the voices of Kayvan Novak, Rosamund Pike and Thomas Brodie-Sangster

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: CITV's Dare Master at Home sees our Dare Master, Danny Murphy, tackle three challenges per episode that you can try for yourself. As always, the cream pie forfeit is waiting should he fail to complete the challenges! The 10-part entertainment series is a scaled down version of Dare Master produced by ITV SignPost under social distancing filming restrictions due to Covid-19.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: After a bad night's sleep, Mike can barely keep his eyes open, but those pesky raccoons keep interrupting his nap.

programme.title: Mighty Mike

programme.title: Mighty Mike

programme.desc: The Great Eduardo, violinist supreme, is in town and Irma wants to go to the concert. But Bean won't pay for the tickets. More than that, he claims that he can play the violin better

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Miss Wince takes Mrs. Wicket off on holiday in her brand-new, swanky caravan; and Bean is left behind. But never one to let a setback knock his confidence, he decides to build his own caravan and sets off in search of the perfect 'quiet spot'. All would be well if he'd only not built the caravan out of Mrs. Wicket's garden she

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis tries to prove she can care for a living creature by rescuing a bad egg

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: The kids try to watch the last episode of their favourite show, Hotel Pennsylvania

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Fuzzypeg eats the Spuds' fish supper and Dave has to catch another. Things look up when he hooks something, but it turns out to be a whale.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: It's the biggest sporting event in Grimsby - the bog roll roll. All Dave has to do is chase the bog roll down the hill and catch it.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Superheroes
programme.desc: Ultron gains control of Iron Man's armour and uses it to wreak havoc on the world, so the Avengers try to stop him and save Iron Man in the process

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Countryfile presenter Anita Rani for the first episode of the new year. Galton Blackiston provides a Michelin-starred new twist on the full English breakfast, and Dipna Anand will be serving up gulab jamun

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner offer tips on what to do with Christmas leftovers. They prepare leek and potato soup served with cheese toastie fingers, vegetarian thatched cottage pies, and sausage orzo served with pangritata. For dessert, they whip up some mini fruit crumbles, using leftover shortbread

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen in Lancashire, where they visit a food hall and a brewery in Clitheroe, as well Lisa's Michelin-starred restaurant Northcote in the village of Langho. Along the way they prepare barbecued John Dory with kohlrabi and fennel salad, Blackburn and Eccles cakes with a selection of cheeses and a classic lamb hot pot

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of the Caribbean, visiting the islands that have inspired his cooking. His first stop is Jamaica, where his father grew up. He visits the island's famous Coronation Market and a Rastafarian farm-to-table restaurant, where he learns to make ackee-stuffed ravioli. He also takes a trip to the villa where Ian Fleming created James Bond

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

Tonight, a man is accidentally tasered, a couple set their kitchen on fire, and a toddler in an electric car runs amok in a shopping centre

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which rugby legend Gareth Thomas, Good Morning Britain presenter Ranvir Singh and comedian London Hughes will need quick wits and nerves of steel to master the arcade-style machine

programme.title: Family Fortunes

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway Presents

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Six unknown celebrities battle it out in the singing contest. The mystery contestants are dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes designed to hide their identities, while the panel - Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Mo Gilligan - tries to guess who they are. In tonight's episode, a fresh batch of Masked Singers battle it out - Harlequin v Blob, Seahorse v Viking and Bush Baby v Grandfather Clock. Joel Dommett hosts the proceedings

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the return of the spinning-chair singing contest, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The four coaches will once again be selecting their teams through blind auditions, where looks play no part in the selection process and each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone

programme.title: Notting Hill (1999)
programme.desc: A recently divorced bookshop owner finds love with a glamorous Hollywood film star, but ends up the focus of unwanted media interest - which makes maintaining a relationship a tricky proposition. programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean accidentally spills all of Mrs. Wicket's new, expensive perfume on the floor. Can he hide the crime and cover his tracks?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean is on his way to the seaside when the mini runs out of petrol. Undeterred he manages to get a push, but for some reason the do-gooders don't want to push him all the way to the beach!

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis wins employee of the month and the right to share breakfast with Aunt Lydia

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Dr Gillman saves Wendy's ponytail and now she's indebted to him

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The Spuds don't care about a carbon footprint, since George has come home with a haul of free electronics that they all want to test out.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave wants to learn to play the drums at school, but is given the recorder. With a talent show around the corner, Dave gets practising.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: In East Sussex, the expert walker sets off from the village of East Dean, as she explores one of the longest undeveloped coastlines on the south coast - one that is disappearing at an erosion rate of almost one meter a year. During her three-hour walk Julia discovers the area's rich social history at seaside settlement Birling Gap, learns of the mystery of Sherlock Holmes' retirement, and hears how a lighthouse had to be shifted 17 metres back from the cliff edge

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh kick-starts the year with joyful January plants and new craft drinks, and welcomes Father Brown's Nancy Carroll and presenter Kirsty Gallacher

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner offer tips on what to do with Christmas leftovers. They prepare leek and potato soup served with cheese toastie fingers, vegetarian thatched cottage pies, and sausage orzo served with pangritata. For dessert, they whip up some mini fruit crumbles, using leftover shortbread

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace, comedian Chris Ramsey and model and presenter Vogue Williams take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray invites three pairs of contestants to call upon their powers of deduction in a battle of words. Using their powers of deduction, they must correctly and quickly work out the words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show, potentially doubling their winnings or leaving with nothing. First up are father and son Simon and Will, competition fanatic Sue and husband Paul, and mother and daughter Dawn and Regan

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts: The Crimes of Grindelwald (2018)
programme.desc: In an attempt to scupper an evil sorcerer's plans of raising pure-blood wizards to rule over all non-magical beings, Albus Dumbledore enlists his former student Newt Scamander, who agrees to help. Second part of JK Rowling's Harry Potter prequel saga, starring Eddie Redmayne, Katherine Waterston and Jude Law

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the game show in which two families guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to play for the £30,000 jackpot. This time, it's the turn of the Craig family from Falkirk, who take on the Mills family from Essex

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns to host the spin-off from The Chase in which contestants take on two, three, four or all five of the quiz's resident experts - Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Paul 'The Sinnerman' Sinha, hoping to win big money against the odds

programme.title: And Finally - With Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The presenter's favourite trademark ‘And Finally' news stories from across his 30-year career at ITN, which offered a glimpse into the nation's lighter, quirkier moments. He catches up with some of the protagonists, including the man who turned his lounge into the Starship Enterprise, and the girl who was christened with 217 names

programme.title: Billy Connolly: It's Been a Pleasure
programme.desc: Celebration of the comedian's greatest performances, made to mark his retirement from stand-up. programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Walks: Top 100
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Ore Oduba present a countdown of the UK's best loved walks, voted for by more than 8,000 enthusiasts. Featuring locations from the West Highland Way to the Ridgeway, and almost everywhere in between. Famous faces including Catherine Tyldesley, Adrian Edmondson, Katherine Kelly, Larry Lamb, Janet Street Porter and Robert Bathurst hit the trails to reveal their own favourite walks, which feature in the poll. They are joined by experts, nature aficionados and members of the public who provide an insight into the hikes, the history and the hidden stories behind the trails

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of seven races fom Exeter, Musselburgh and Tramore The highlight is the Savills New Year Chase at Tramore, which features the return of dual Gold Cup winner Al Boum Photo in a fascinating contest. There are also competitive handicaps across the card from Exeter and Musselburgh. With analysis from AP McCoy and Luke Harvey, betting news from Rishi Persad, reporting from Alice Plunkett and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Oli, Sophie, Cina and Bob pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Harriet struggles, Dawn panics, and it remains to be seen whether Paul will open up to Mandy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Craig's intervention leads to Gary's arrest, Dev angers his neighbours by fraternising with Ray, and Steve shares a fundraising idea in Oliver's memory

programme.title: Dancing on Thin Ice with Torvill & Dean
programme.desc: Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean fulfil a lifelong dream by travelling to Alaska to skate on natural ice, having only ever performed on artificially created rinks in their 45 years dancing together. Travelling by train and husky sled, they encounter first-hand the dramatic effects of climate change on the wilderness. Alaska's hottest year on record means there is not a lot of ice to be found, so Jayne and Chris's trek becomes a hunt, skating wherever they can hoping to find a magnificent natural setting to dance a special new version of their iconic Bolero. Narrated by Stephen Fry

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Pitch Perfect (2012)
programme.desc: A new student finds herself a social outcast during her first year of college, but soon acquires a new circle of friends in a band of misfits with a shared talent for singing. She joins their a cappella group and leads them to compete in the campus music competitions. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Rebel Wilson and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh meets his match in former international CIA agent Wesley Cole (Seann William Scott), who returns home and assumes duty as an LAPD officer. Cole soon finds himself navigating a new partnership with Murtaugh as he adjusts to life in Los Angeles, while Avery continues his campaign for city council. With Damon Wayans, Keesha Sharp and Kevin Rahm

programme.desc: CITV's Dare Master at Home sees our Dare Master, Danny Murphy, tackle three challenges per episode that you can try for yourself. The 10-part entertainment series is a scaled down version of Dare Master produced by ITV SignPost under social distancing filming restrictions due to Covid-19.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: After a bad night's sleep, Mike can barely keep his eyes open, but those pesky raccoons keep interrupting his nap.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
