programme.desc: Kate Quilton learns some important differences between gelato and ice-cream at a parlour in Rome, while Jimmy Doherty visits Mauritius to find out about white and dark rum and learns where the term 'proof' originally came from in relation to alcohol. Matt Tebbutt visits a traditional sweet factory in Blackburn to examine why his mouth goes cold when he sucks on a mint

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: With retirement rapidly approaching, Jan and Phil Jackson wish to relocate to the Canary Island of Fuerteventura, having been inspired by Jan's sister who has already made the move there. Property expert Scarlette Douglas shows them five detached villas within their £215,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Renuka Joseph is looking for a holiday home where she can entertain family and friends and relax with her husband Clarence. She's originally from Sri Lanka and has chosen the Costa del Sol as her perfect spot because the beaches remind her of home. She's brought her best friend Shanez along to help, so Jasmine Harman shows them five apartments in and around Estepona for her £80,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: A Redditch couple want to spend some time in the sun now their four children have grown up, so Laura Hamilton helps them search for an apartment in the Spanish town of Torrevieja

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two young couples who were struggling to take their first step onto the property ladder. He was in Leeds with Charlie and Hayley, who had been searching fruitlessly for nine months, unable to make a decision for fear of making the wrong one. In south-east London, Nicki and Rob wanted somewhere big enough to start a family when Kirstie and Phil suggested an unusual solution. How are both couples getting on now?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two young house-hunting couples in Bristol and Hertfordshire, to see how their lives have changed since they first met. Back in 2016, childhood sweethearts Rich and Bethan were searching for their first marital home together, and wanted Phil's help to finally seal a deal. In the same year, Phil also helped Laura and Ben relocate to Birmingham from Bristol

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five cooks from in and around Harrogate, North Yorkshire, battle to win the £1,000 prize in the culinary contest. First to take on hosting duties is farm girl Jane Steele, who hopes a combination of innovative recipes and fancy wine will win over her guests - horse enthusiast Cassy Bell, photographer Simon Meyer, country gent Alasdair Moore and adventurous punk and cupcake seller Cathy McConaghy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Cassy Bell hosts the second dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, hoping her jockey-themed evening and gastro-pub menu will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cathy McConaghy hosts the third dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where she decides to return to her Irish roots and serve a menu of home-made soda bread, cockle soup, oysters, roast lamb, and stout-infused sticky toffee pudding as she vies for the £1,000 prize. However, not all of her guests are impressed with the choice of food being dished up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Simon Meyer is the fourth dinner-party host in Harrogate, North Yorkshire. He takes a laid-back approach to the task, but his chicken soup starter goes down so well the guests' comments leave him struggling to retain his composure

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Country gent Alasdair Moore hosts the final dinner party in Harrogate, and hopes to impress his guests with his specially selected wines and 'proper' menu. While his monkfish is well received, an early slip-up dents his confidence, and a risky parlour game does not endear him to farm lady Jane Steele. As the evening progresses, his competitors rate their experience, before the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the School House B&B in Pencader, Carmarthenshire, where owners Jayne and Ian Hall-Edwards hope their environmental approach will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Cathedral 73 in Cardiff, managed by Neil Chidzey. On arrival, guests are wowed by the glamorous rooms, but Raj is shocked to find breakfast is not included

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Leafy Suburban B&B in Northwood, Hertfordshire, where the guests spend the afternoon taking a swing at some golfing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to the Wellington Inn, Hertfordshire, where Felicity Rainbow takes a spiritual approach, cleansing the aura of the rooms and encouraging guests to feel the vibe

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The School House B&B is first and host Jayne Hall-Edwards takes guests back to basics over her green policy. Next, Neil Chidzey and Jo Goodwin from Cathedral 73 challenge their guests over costings

programme.title: The Plane That Saved Britain
programme.desc: Paralympics presenter, former Royal Marines commando and pilot Arthur Williams presents this love letter to the Second World War aeroplane he believes history has unjustly forgotten. While the names Spitfire, Lancaster and Hurricane have passed into legend, the De Havilland Mosquito languishes in relative obscurity. But for Arthur, the 'Wooden Wonder' is the plane that saved Britain. Here he meets the men who flew it, tells its extraordinary story and travels to Virginia Beach in the US to see if he can take to the skies in the world's only remaining flying Mosquito

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow 56-year-old Dave, who is brought to St George's after falling from a ladder at work. Doctors are concerned that he may have life-changing damage to his spine. Meanwhile, three-year-old Alf arrives in resus with his parents after being hit by a car

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras reveal how individuals adapt and survive in the face of adversity. Subjects include Paul, 60, who is rushed to A&E after colliding with a van. Doctors are concerned that he may have serious injuries to his pelvis and spine. There's also a profile of four-year-old Jeremy, who is struggling to breathe. He suffers from a series of chronic conditions, including microcephaly and epilepsy

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Key striker Father Jack drinks himself into oblivion just as the Craggy Island clerics are about to play a vital five-a-side football match against their Rugged Island rivals, led by devious Dick Byrne. Dermot Morgan stars

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect heads off to Italy, where it seems the residents have also been bitten by the 'small buildings are beautiful' bug. The most spectacular place he visits is so high up in the mountains that it's only accessible by helicopter, but this difficulty is more than made up for by the views seen from it. Back in Britain, George meets a man turning a wrecked private jet into a crash pad and a tattoo artist with big plans for an old railway carriage. George also challenges his pal, craftsman Will Hardie, to create a woodland retreat for less than the price of a family holiday

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: George is joined by leading architects to help transform some of Britain's most unloved houses into desirable properties, beginning with the 1970s home of Tony and Sarah in the Vale of Glamorgan. The couple want rid of the pebbledash facade, small windows and dated decor, to create a great family home on a relatively modest budget of £60,000. They receive some bespoke and cutting-edge input from commercial and residential architect Greg Blee, with a plan that will link their home to the landscape beyond, including a Shard-like extension

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The presenter attempts to unlock the potential of old houses, including the rules when it comes to putting a kitchen in a period home. He also meets a woman who bought a 400-year-old Devon cottage, and has some solutions for turning this old house into a new home

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Francis Leigh hosts the third dinner party in Winchester, hoping to dazzle his guests with his sunburnt chicken curry, a Thai meal with a twist and a dessert simply called 'sticky.' programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent William Tilsley throws one last dinner party as a week of competitions in Winchester, Hampshire, draws to a close. As the night draws to a close, one of the competitors rises above the rest to become the winner

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Thompson Hall Retreat in Norfolk, boasting stylish shepherd huts, and run by mother and daughter duo Amanda Benton and Becci Howlett

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Clairvoyant Yvette Tamara hosts the first dinner party in north Kent, and the night gets off to a spooky start when data analyst John Tower and working mother Yemi Christopher-Soares discover a ghost-busting machine. Meanwhile, jeweller Lisa Townend is so impressed with Yvette's abilities that she wants to return for a psychic reading

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Data analyst John Tower hosts the second dinner party in north Kent, and lets his passion for military re-enactment show when he barks orders at the table. However, not all the guests enjoy his fatty pork stew and psychedelic dessert, and two argue over dating etiquette

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Project manager Yemi Christopher-Soares hosts the third dinner party in north Kent, where she hopes the guests will enjoy her Nigerian-inspired menu and appreciate her love of all things spicy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Lisa Townend hosts the final dinner party in north Kent, and hopes that a menu full of French fancies and innovative entertainment in the form of human roulette, will be enough to impress her guests and see her crowned winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of private chef Ed Versluys and pilates instructor Vicky Anderson, who want to convert a concrete cowshed in the Somerset countryside into a three-bedroom home. With the help of one young builder and the knowledge they can learn from the internet, the couple plan to project manage the conversion themselves. However, they have only eight months and a budget of just over £200k to make a warm and comfortable home with straw bale walls and wide expanses of glass

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: After falling over during the night at home and being rushed into A&E, 85-year-old Maura faces potentially life-changing injuries. CT scans reveal broken bones in her spine, and her son and daughter-in-law must decide when to share the gravity of the situation with Maura and her granddaughter Meg. Meanwhile, 37-year-old doctor Jim arrives at St George's after a collision with a van while he was cycling to work. Despite putting on a brave face, scans reveal his injuries are more serious than first thought. Plus, a chef is treated after cutting her thumb while chopping peppers at home

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Eighty-year-old Rose has fluid on her lungs and is having trouble breathing. Her daughters Christine, Sandra and Debbie put on brave faces around her bedside, convincing her that everything will be all right, but in the relatives' room there's high emotion as they contemplate life without their mother. Also in resus at King's College Hospital is Kevin, a 55-year-old trucker who has sustained injuries in a road accident

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The crews are stretched to their limits as they deal with a 19-year-old man who has suffered a motorbike crash, and an elderly woman showing signs of a stroke but refusing treatment as it means she will leave behind her beloved husband of over 40 years

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow patients at south west London's St George's hospital on royal wedding day. As Harry and Meghan tie the knot, patients and their relatives reflect on the meaning of lifelong love, devotion and commitment. Among those treated are David, 74, who is flown to St George's by air ambulance after falling from a ladder. Medics suspect internal bleeding and he is rushed to CT to assess the damage. His wife Lynda makes her way to the hospital as doctors try to prevent catastrophic blood loss

The event means a big reunion for the gang, at which Smithy receives surprising news from Nessa, and Stacey has a special request for her husband

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Dave is unhappy about Nessa's plans to let Smithy see the baby, while Stacey ensures Gavin sticks to his promise as she tries to find a new job. Meanwhile, Dawn and Pete join Pam and Mick for a beer and curry night. Comedy, starring Joanna Page and Mathew Horne

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Everyone gathers in Billericay as Pete and Dawn prepare to renew their wedding vows. Meanwhile, Smithy goes beyond the call of friendship when he makes a staggering suggestion

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Bank Holiday Monday sees the Essex contingent arrive for a sunny day at the beach in Barry. Gavin has a heart-to-heart with his dad, which gives him much to think about, while a dark cloud looms on the horizon when Dave arrives to find Nessa and Smithy enjoying all the fun of the fair. Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden and Ruth Jones star

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Preparations for Nessa and Dave's wedding are in full swing, Stacey has life-changing news for Gavin, and Bryn and Jason put their big secret to bed once and for all. Finally, the wedding arrives and, as Nessa prepares to make her way to the church, Smithy takes the opportunity to have one last chat. Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden, Ruth Jones and Rob Brydon star

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Johnny Vegas accompanies Richard Ayoade on a brief jaunt to the United Arab Emirates and the oil-rich city of Dubai. Richard checks into a suite boasting five balconies at the Atlantis Hotel, on the world's largest man-made island. The luxury continues with a city tour by seaplane and a taste of the world's most expensive ice-cream. The pair enter the biggest shopping centre in the world, where they enjoy a camel sandwich and a camelccino, and visit the observation deck of the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade takes comedian Roisin Conaty to Berlin for a two-day cultural break. The duo check-in at the vibrant nhow music hotel, which boasts two in-house recording studios with instruments available on room service. The mini-breakers sign-up for a guided city tour in an old East German Trabant car, finishing at Checkpoint Charlie. They also step inside a replica Cold War apartment, and visit a beer garden in the city's most popular park

programme.title: Not Going Out

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: The workshop struggles to convert three quad bikes into frontline police vehicles, while an officer has to crash a pursuit car to protect the public. Steve wants to share his home-made birthday cake - if only he can persuade someone to eat it

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: Police officers request a new police support unit or riot van, while a stolen car chase ends with an X5 wedged in a hedge. Boss Mick Sweetmore goes back to the floor with Merseyside's mental health team and discovers another side to modern-day policing - as well as the poor state of the vehicle they are currently using

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The hosts bid to prove that cars can be art by filling a gallery in Middlesbrough with motoring-related works, including a few creations of their own. They also resort to unusual promotional techniques to attract more visitors to their exhibition. Back on the track, Jeremy Clarkson tests British supercar the Noble M600. Jenson Button drives the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compete in a 1950s-style race from London to Edinburgh. On the footplate of the A1 Tornado steam train, Jeremy has to feed the boiler with a constant supply of coal, while Richard braves the saddle of a Vincent Black Shadow motorbike and James drives an old Jaguar XK120 as the trio discover what Top Gear might have been like 60 years ago. Michael Schumacher drives the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond, Jeremy Clarkson and James May try to find the perfect car for 17-year-olds, dealing with insurance companies, accident repairs and a muddy music festival car park in the process. Richard also visits Abu Dhabi to test the Lamborghini Murcielago SV and stages a race between the 1000hp Bugatti Veyron and the McLaren F1. Actor, writer and comedian Stephen Fry is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy accidentally runs over a pet rabbit belonging to the daughter of an important client, so she needs someone to help cover her tracks. Unfortunately, the best people she has are Lee and Daisy. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by actor Jon Hamm, star of Mad Men and Baby Driver, for a yuletide trek around Hong Kong - the most visited and most vertical city on the planet. They join the other 26.7 million tourists, as they gorge themselves on questionable foodstuffs, traditional transportation and the world's largest seated Buddha statue. After resting at the Peninsula Hotel for some colonial opulence, they sample street food and get their fortunes read, before taking to the air for a bird's eye view by helicopter

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall: Unpacked
programme.desc: A look at some of the moments that nearly stopped the Expedition teams in their tracks, as well as the points where taking one step further meant no chance of turning back

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: One half of The Mighty Boosh and co-host of The Great British Bake Off Noel Fielding takes over the hosting reins of the long-running show as he comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The comedian, actor, artist, DJ and musician treats the audience to his own peculiar brand of surreal humour, before introducing routines by two up-and-coming stars of the comedy circuit, with south Londoner Dane Baptiste and Irishman Al Porter taking to the stage at the famous venue

programme.title: Hypothetical
programme.desc: Comedy game show hosted by Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster, in which leading comedians are posed increasingly absurd hypothetical situations and scored on how well they would deal with them. The first episode features Tom Allen, Rob Beckett, Jessica Knappett and Liza Tarbuck

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Bob Mortimer, Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A colossal American football stadium in Texas, that features the world's largest retractable roof and is able to seat over 100,000 spectators with no restricted viewpoints

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Examining a fleet of cruise ships that are the largest passenger vessels in the world, and revealing how they have built on 19th-century experiments in hydrodynamics

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at how corruption became rife within the SS, with the money stolen from Jews finding its way into the pockets of the camp guards rather than funding the war effort. Many of the SS also enjoyed illicit sexual relationships with female prisoners. Meanwhile, Dr Mengele embarked on a gruesome series of experiments, using children as guinea pigs in his research into genetic inheritance

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The events of 1944, when the death toll rose substantially. By this point, the Allies were aware of Auschwitz's purpose, and faced a difficult decision over what should be done about it. Plus, the story of the occupation of Hungary, revealing how Adolf Eichmann offered to spare a million Jews in return for certain services

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The final episode examines the liberation of Auschwitz and other camps by the Soviet Red Army. However, freedom did not mean an end to the prisoners' suffering - many were mistreated by their liberators and thousands died for the want of medical treatment

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The untold secret story of war production that shaped the Second World War, beginning with how the German aviation industry came to dominate the skies of Europe

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The story behind the Krupp company, who were responsible for the manufacture of two of Germany's most feared war products - the Panzer Tank and the U-boat

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The role played by engineering giant Vickers during the Second World War, including the development of the Spitfire and how the Royal Navy recovered from losing so many warships

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife inhabiting underwater kelp forests, including thousands of giant cuttlefish spawning along a restricted area of rocky reef off the south coast of Australia. Males outnumber females 11 to one, which leads to fierce competition. Larger males use brute force to drive off competition, while their smaller rivals use deception by mimicking the appearance of females. The programme also features tiger sharks hunting for green turtles in fields of seagrass and spider crabs trying to avoid predators while they shed their shells

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the ways human activity can impact on aquatic wildlife, including albatrosses unwittingly feeding their offspring plastic, mother dolphins with contaminated milk, and communications between animals being disrupted by noise. However, there are signs of hope, including the thriving Norwegian Spring-Spawning Herring, previously on the brink of extinction, and efforts to save endangered species like the whale shark and to preserve the eggs of giant leatherback turtles

programme.title: Your Favourite Attenborough Moment
programme.desc: Countdown of viewers' 20 favourite clips of veteran naturalist David Attenborough in action, including comments from the broadcaster himself

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines two creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world. The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There is a triple collection of desirable cars in store for Derek when an MG Midget, an Austin-Healey 'frogeye' Sprite and an RS2000 come up for auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave's son Jack is heading out to Cheshire to collect two cars with a 35-year age difference. And luckily for Mathewsons, Lucy knows exactly what has been brought in for auction

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits Swindon's railway village, which became home to Great Western's enormous works and he also explores the dark and secret past of Milan's station

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The mysterious Cachtice Castle home to Elizabeth Bathory, said to have killed over 600 girls, and a mysterious structure on the 38th parallel

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: Exploring the military headquarters built beneath Dover Castle during the Second World War and Odessa's catacombs, possibly the largest tunnel network in the world

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Romania to discover the truth behind vampire mythology and Bram Stoker's Dracula, uncovering the history of Vlad the Impaler and Elizabeth Bathory

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Over the centuries, the established religions and their various offshoots have been the subject of much controversy, debate and argument that continues right up to the present day. Jamie Theakston looks beyond the recognised orthodoxies and beliefs as he goes - literally and figuratively - underground to explore the extraordinary history of the Church of Satan

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a North Yorkshire-based, family-run historic car auction business, which uncovers some remarkably rare vehicles in the picturesque village of Thornton-le-Dale

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a Bradford-built 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Paul and Dave uncover a Ford Escort that's been untouched for 30 years

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his late father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Mathewsons agree to sell a vintage threshing machine and large collection of tractors, but extracting them from old barns on a Yorkshire farm proves a bigger job than anticipated

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A Cold War Soviet classic goes under the hammer. Built like a tank, the Moskovitch gets few admirers for style, but finds a loving home with a fan of Eastern Bloc classics

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns more about the country's railways, beginning with the Great Eastern Line, which runs along the edge of England and into central London. On his travels, the former politician discovers the grave-robbing history of Great Yarmouth and tries his hand at working a Victorian swing bridge in Reedham, Norfolk. He also takes to the air to reveal how a rail guidebook helped aviators during the Second World War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey along the Great Eastern Line, which ventures from the edge of England to the heart of London. In this leg, he examines the Victorians' fascination with the Suffolk village of Dunwich, Britain's answer to Atlantis, and discovers how nearby Felixstowe became home to the UK's biggest container port

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the sites of Victorian freak shows, rides one of the world's first electric railways in Southend and encounters the preserved head of a medieval politician as he continues his journey along the route of the Great Eastern Line

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Epping, Essex, to Hackney in north-east London, discovering why dairy herds were transported by train all over Britain. He also finds out the importance of the gunpowder trade to Waltham Cross, and learns the gruesome details of the first murder to take place on a train

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues to follow the route of the Great Eastern Line, enjoying a ride on a miniature railway hidden beneath London. During his trip, he also rings the bells of Bow Church, and tries his hand at announcing at Fenchurch Street station

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo retraces railway journeys made by Queen Victoria in the south of England, where she would travel from Windsor Castle in Berkshire to her summer retreat on the Isle of Wight. The first leg of the trip takes him from Windsor's railway station to Didcot in Oxfordshire, stopping along the way to view a structure designed by Isambard Kingdom Brunel in Maidenhead. He also tries to collect the mail in the manner of Victorian postal workers on a steam-powered travelling post office

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Roy Clarke's comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason. Tight-fisted shopkeeper Arkwright purchases fire-damaged stock, only to find all the tins have lost their labels

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to persuade Arkwright to buy a van, as he is fed up with having to struggle doing the rounds with the shop's outdated delivery bike. He is pleasantly surprised when his skinflint uncle agrees - until he takes the vehicle out on the road. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright feels neglected by Nurse Gladys Emmanuel, and decides to feign an illness in an attempt to get her undivided attention, but she soon sees through the ruse

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A rare chance to see some memorable performances from the first 12 years of Top of the Pops, with the Rolling Stones, Tom Jones, Stevie Wonder, Queen, the Kinks and Status Quo

programme.title: David Bowie: Finding Fame
programme.desc: The final part of Francis Whately's trilogy of films on Bowie's life, telling the story of the five years when many of the ideas that would help him become an icon were first born. Starting in 1966, soon after David Jones changed his name to David Bowie, the film traces his interest in everything from Holst and Pinky and Perky to Anthony Newley and Tibetan Buddhism, and how he used all of these influences to create not only Ziggy Stardust, but the material for his entire career

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How Japan's early victories over Allied forces during the Second World War convinced its people that their strength and discipline would bring them victory. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Allied offensive in the Pacific, where America's two-pronged attack led to heavy casualties on both sides, as the Japanese mounted a ferocious defence. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Enola Gay drops the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima in August 1945 as America bids to secure an unconditional surrender from the Japanese, even though conventional bombing raids had already flattened the country. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The award-winning documentary series reveals how the end of the Second World War resulted in Britain's military and economic exhaustion, leaving Europe at the mercy of sparring superpowers as the Cold War era dawned. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A fuel plant in Poland built to make Nazi Germany self-sufficient by using the latest technology of the time to turn coal into oil

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of a set of Second World War sea defences built near the mouth of the River Thames to protect London. Plus, a concrete pyramid build during the Cold War

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of the Vajont Dam in the mountains of northern Italy, and the catastrophic flood in 1963 that caused the deaths of 2000 people

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The stories behind an abandoned Second World War aviation facility in Farnborough, a Soviet airbase in the Gobi Desert, and a disused furnace in Pittsburgh

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle's cunning plan to release the airmen from the drain in the square is ruined when the gypsy fair is cancelled. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye and Kirsten Cooke

programme.title: Britain's Bestselling Toys
programme.desc: Keith Lemon presents this countdown of the greatest playthings of the past 50 years, remembering the Christmas must-haves and big crazes from the 1970s to the present day. programme.title: Chitty Flies Again with David Walliams
programme.desc: To celebrate the 50th anniversary of the iconic musical film Chitty Chitty Bang Bang, the comedy actor, children's author and die-hard Chitty fan attempts an engineering feat like no other - bring the magic of this timeless classic to life by building a real-life Chitty Chitty Bang Bang car. To help him in this world first, madcap experiment, he recruits aircraft engineer Tony Hoskins and a class of enthusiastic eight-year-old pupils. Along the way David delves into the history of this timeless gem, from its inception by James Bond author Ian Fleming, to its adaption to the screen by literary legend Roald Dahl

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Polish-born carer Sylwia hosts the fourth party of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, and hopes her European menu will charm her guests. However, the growing friction between diners Annika and Carolyn threatens to spoil the evening, and the host's chances of winning the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party night in Boston, and the hard-to-please foxhunter Carolyn is planning on using a charm offensive to win over the guests after having criticised each of them during the week. She has offended Annika, called Katy fake, found fault with Sywia's food, and reprimanded Adie, but still believes she may be able to turn things around

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Donna and Martin, from Dagenham, east London, seek a holiday home for the whole family to enjoy on the stunning Costa Dorada in Spain. They have a budget of £65,000 and want a one-bedroom apartment with outside space close to the bustling town of Salou. Jasmine Harman lines up five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Cliff and Angela from Nottingham fell in love with St Lucia when they were on a Caribbean cruise, and now, after discovering their pensions aren't performing as well as they'd like, they've decided to invest in a property there. Can Jasmine Harman find a place that will be a sound investment and also give them and their family an idyllic holiday home for years to come, all for their £120,000 budget?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Fay Lewis has dreamed of moving to Spain for 20 years. Now, with her house in the UK sold and her bags packed, she's ready to take the plunge. Jasmine Harman shows Fay five properties around the Axarquia region of the Costa del Sol for her budget of 95,000 euros

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: B&B operators Linda and Mike are looking for a holiday home to use as a winter retreat while their business is closed. Linda fell in love with Antigua 20 years ago and is taking Mike along for the first time on a property-finding mission. Scarlette Douglas shows the couple five potential properties within their £260,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ian Snowley and Kay Marriot fell in love with the volcanic landscapes and coasts of Lanzarote, and are now determined to buy a house that they can use to explore the island further. Scarlette Douglas shows the couple five properties, hoping to maximise their budget of £150,000

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a mother-and-son team who have gone through 20 chefs in two years at their struggling Italian restaurant in California. The adjoining bar is a triumph, but the oldest dining establishment in town needs an overhaul if the inexperienced owners are to make a profit

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay's next stop is Philadelphia, where family run restaurant Chiarella's is struggling to survive in an area full of thriving Italian eateries. Tommy and Dina Defino's marriage is on the verge of collapse and his sense of personal failure only adds to the troubles of their crisis-ridden business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: A round-up of memorable moments from the show over the course of the year, as armchair critics including Giles and Mary, Jenny and Lee, Ellie and Izzi, Mary and Marina, the Siddiquis, and Amira and Iqra give their thoughts on the year's TV output

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding, Matt Lucas, Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood get the New Year celebrations started in the tent. Joining them are Helena and Henry from 2019, Nancy from 2014, and 2018 winner Rahul, all hoping to impress the judges with their skills and creativity and take home the coveted Festive Star Baker title

programme.title: The Crystal Maze
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is the man in charge as singer Fleur East, former Queen of the Jungle Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo and actors John Thomson, Nina Wadia and Natalie Cassidy take on everything the maze throws at them. As always, they face an assortment of skill, physical, mystery and mental challenges in their bid to win as much time as possible in the Crystal Dome

programme.title: Britain's Wildest Weather
programme.desc: Footage of the most extreme weather conditions in the UK over the past year, from the worst floods in a decade and a freak landslide in Fife, to an account of being in a house when it is struck by lightning. The programme also explains the scientific reasons behind the wildfire that destroyed one of the UK's most precious nature reserves, as well as a look back at record-breaking days of both heat and rain

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Sam Smith and Joe Wicks join Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty for an early New Year celebration. On the menu tonight are Italian-inspired pork and crispy double crackling, laid on a bed of veg and creamy beans and topped with a tangy herb salsa. Sam also contributes a childhood favourite food, blending the flavours of Mexico and Scotland - haggis tacos complete with home-made corn tortillas, red onion pickle, salsa and avocado, while Joe gets a masterclass in making fresh pasta

programme.title: Celebrity Snoop Dogs
programme.desc: A special festive edition of the show in which celebrities' dogs provide a unique perspective on their homes. The special twist this time is that celebrity dog owners Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Gok Wan and Joanna Page are watching on and competing against each other to throw the others off the scent as their four-legged friends roam around their lavishly decorated homes

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The story of Lesley and Mark Irwin, who fell foul of a single mother and confidence trickster. After several months, the tenant fell behind with the rent and it was discovered she had advertised the home as a rental property and stole deposits from unwitting students hoping to move into the area. Plus, a family living in a cramped and cold multiple-occupancy house in south London, and have to share a bathroom and kitchen with other tenants, seek help from Lewisham Council

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A couple of landlords fall foul of two young YouTube stars who are using their rental property as a film set - and to make matters worse, the tenants have stopped paying their rent but are refusing to leave. In Chelmsford, eviction lawyer Chris receives a shock when he tries to evict a group of squatters from an abandoned printworks, and in Hampstead, London, a landlord investigates when neighbours complain about noise from one of his flats

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A landlord offers a former tenant the use of a spare room until she gets back on her feet, but then struggles to evict her - and things get even more difficult when the woman moves her partner in. A landlord travelling abroad has to return to London when her tenants stop paying the rent, and Harrow Council officers find 30 mattresses in a flat designed for four people

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Stavros and Emily try to get their house back from squatters, while Margaret attempts to recover the £20,000 she is owed from a letting agent, and Peterborough council find 12 people crammed into a category-one fire hazard house. Documentary series in which cameras follow the victims and villains of the buy-to-let boom

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Landlord Nicholas Parrington discovers a couple are subletting his house, just months after a previous tenant had turned the property into a brothel, while investors in a community centre for children are aghast when squatters move into their building. In Lewisham, the council's Rogue Landlord Unit raids one of their main streets, on the lookout for slum conditions, illegal overcrowding and criminal activities

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In Staffordshire agents try to collect nearly £2,500 in unpaid parking fines, while officers Matt and Garry head to the West Midlands to recover £4,500 owed from a debtor to his former girlfriend. Agents travel to south-east London looking to collect £3,000 owed by a taxi company

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south-east London, officers Gary and Cona chase a debt of nearly £50,000 in unpaid rent, leading to a feisty confrontation with the debtor. Agents Gary and Paul try to recover over £2,000 owed by a teenager to a local garage in Kent, and officers Max and Paul head to south-west London on the trail of a film producer who owes £8,000 to financial advisers

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In Coventry, the sheriffs chase £10,000 owed by a publican to a contractor. The property appears to be empty, but after finding an ingenious method of entry, Matt and Garry are confronted by a surprised and angry landlord. Meanwhile, agents Max and Steve head to central London to try to recover almost £8,000 owed by a former peer of the realm to a firm of accountants

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Featuring two quarrelling neighbours who record each other's every moment on CCTV and in diaries. programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A man living in Cumbria takes issue with his neighbour's habit of letting his menagerie of chickens, cockerels, parrots and other birds freely wander along a residential road, and is even more disturbed by the fact that some of his fellow residents don't seem to have a problem with it. A former urban couple who moved to a rural valley in north Wales to breed horses enter open conflict with the owners of a nearby yurt business, whose fireworks parties spooked one of their prized beasts, resulting in an injury. Finally, a Romanian woman finds herself subject to abuse when she tries to set up a new home with her daughter in Wigan

programme.desc: A tenacious TV producer sets out to get to the bottom of a friend's murder, certain that the killer was a man her pal had met online. However, it soon becomes clear that her very personal investigation has served only to place her in danger as well. Mystery, based on Mary Higgins Clark's novel, starring Patsy Kensit, Cynthia Preston and Dean McDermott

programme.title: Lucky Day (2002)
programme.desc: An aspiring actress is shocked when a friend is found dead just days after winning the lottery. Ignoring the warnings of police, she vows to get to the bottom of his murder herself. But her foolhardy inquisitiveness only serves to place her in peril as the trail leads to a familiar face. Thriller, adapted from a novel by Mary Higgins Clark, starring Amanda Donohoe, Gino Marrocco and Gregor Torzs

Suspecting she may have been the target, the journalist races against time to solve the mystery and escape the deadly attentions of the enraged assassin. Thriller, starring Alison Eastwood, Bo Svenson, Margot Kidder and Iris Quinn

programme.title: Columbo: Dead Weight (1971)
programme.desc: The dishevelled detective suspects a highly respected general is guilty of murdering an associate who was about to expose the war hero's shady business dealings, but Columbo has a hard time backing up his hunch with evidence because of the lack of a body or a weapon. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Eddie Albert, Suzanne Pleshette and John Kerr

programme.title: Columbo: Murder by the Book (1971)
programme.desc: An author of mystery novels kills his former writing partner so he can claim sole credit for their collaborations, and tries to appear innocent by offering to help Columbo solve the crime. Detective drama, directed by Steven Spielberg, starring Peter Falk, Jack Cassidy, Rosemary Forsyth and Barbara Colby

programme.title: Columbo: Playback (1975)
programme.desc: An inventor tries to stop his mother-in-law from exposing his infidelities by murdering her in front of a security camera - and tampering with the recording to make it look like the killing occurred at a time when he had an alibi. However, Columbo sees through his hi-tech ruse and tries to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Oskar Werner and Gena Rowlands

programme.title: Columbo: Double Exposure (1973)
programme.desc: A maker of motivational films based on subliminal messages uses his expertise to plant a subconscious command in the mind of a rival to lure him to his death. However, any hopes of his getting away with it are dashed by the arrival of a certain shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Culp, Robert Middleton, Louise Latham and Arlene Martel

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate an assault on a restaurateur's wife, and find the attack is similar to a rape committed the previous week. They discover a locksmith visited both women's houses before the incident, but when they examine the stolen goods found in his toolbox, a new connection emerges - putting another suspect in the frame. Crime drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Detectives Benson and Stabler check the handbag of a suspected rape victim for identification and discover the severed finger of a small child in a plastic bag. A murder investigation is launched and Tutuola uncovers a series of traumatic events experienced by the woman in question during childhood

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman asks Benson and Stabler to help her find out who made her daughter pregnant. The girl, who has Down's syndrome, is unable to give much information, especially while being pressured by her distraught mother to abort the baby

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The murder of a transvestite in a church throws suspicion on its resident priest, who Benson and Stabler discover has a history of sexual abuse charges. They try to talk to the cleric's previous victims, who are reluctant to get involved because of a confidentiality agreement they signed - but when one of them finally opens up, new light is thrown on the culprit's identity

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team's search for a serial rapist leads to a suspect who as a child was the victim of the same type of crime at the hands of his father - prompting his lawyer to cite genetics as the reason for his actions. Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek star

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Foster parents are accused of murdering a five-year-old in their care, but it seems their son's video game obsession could be the real key to her death - until the victim's biological mother snatches her other daughters from the house. Hard-hitting police drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.desc: A detective's investigation into a series of murders prompts her to return to her home town. Though she is initially determined to find out whether the killings are linked to a tragic event years earlier, her misgivings begin to grow - and she realises that to solve the crimes she must face her own troubled past. Drama adapted from a tale by Mary Higgins Clark, starring Gabrielle Anwar, Max Martini and Gary Chalk

programme.title: Columbo: The Conspirators (1978)
programme.desc: A terrorist posing as an Irish author heads a peace organization while secretly running guns for the IRA in Belfast. When he kills an arms dealer who tried to swindle him, the writer is sure he has covered his tracks - until Columbo shambles on to the scene. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Clive Revill, Jeanette Nolan and Bernard Behrens

programme.title: Columbo: Make Me a Perfect Murder (1978)
programme.desc: A TV executive's assistant responds to being denied a promotion by killing her boss, then uses her years of experience working on detective shows to create the perfect cover-up. But she reckons without the intervention of Columbo, who begins laying his trap for her while also nursing a neck injury. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and Trish Van Devere

programme.title: Columbo: By Dawn's Early Light (1974)
programme.desc: The commandant of a military academy is outraged by the chairman of the board's plans to admit female cadets, so he stages a fatal accident involving a ceremonial cannon. However, Columbo is not fooled by the deception and sets out to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Patrick McGoohan, Tom Simcox and Mark Wheeler

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The detectives are concerned to hear a convict charged with the rape and murder of a prostitute has been paroled, and decide to keep tabs on him. They trace their suspect to a hotel where gunshots are heard, and find a badly beaten prostitute holding the weapon. However, their belief that she was acting in self-defence is soon overturned. Drama, with Christopher Meloni

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is accused of abusing her teenage stepson - who is later implicated in a murder. As investigations proceed, she goes to great lengths to protect him and prevent the detectives learning the truth. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An elderly woman suffering from Alzheimer's disease breaks into an apartment. Discovering she has been abused, the detectives learn she was released from care to the custody of her scheming son, who becomes the prime suspect. However, shocking revelations about the local nursing home soon come to light

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead following what appears to have been an auto-erotic encounter, Benson and Stabler make a link with the case of a male stripper claiming to have been raped during a hen night. While Cabot prosecutes the sexual assault case, the detectives find that the girl's seemingly accidental death was actually murder. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler is personally affected by the team's investigation into the case of a woman whose unborn child was removed from her body via a crude Caesarean section two months before its due date. As inquiries proceed, he uncovers a tangled tale of infidelity and questions about the paternity of the baby. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler try to track down a mother when her injured six-year-old son is abandoned following a shooting - but a second attack on the child leads them to believe the woman could also be in danger from the would-be killer. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: Keaton and the CoIntel team must locate a domestic target before Chigorin launches an all-out assault on unsuspecting innocents

programme.title: Columbo: Swan Song (1974)
programme.desc: A country singer arranges for his wife and his underage lover to both die in a phoney plane crash after his spouse diverts all his money to her religious causes in revenge for his affair. He is confident he remains above suspicion, but Columbo realises something does not add up and sets out to wrest a confession from the musician. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Johnny Cash and Ida Lupino

programme.title: Columbo: Troubled Waters (1975)
programme.desc: The slovenly sleuth sets out on a pleasure cruise to Acapulco. However, one of the passengers is planning to kill his lover to stop her blackmailing him, and has arranged a cast-iron alibi. But when the crime is discovered, the captain looks to Columbo to solve the mystery. Drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Vaughn, Dean Stockwell, Patrick Macnee and Poupee Bocar

programme.title: Columbo: It's All in the Game (1993)
programme.desc: A wealthy socialite and her daughter discover they have been seeing the same man, and have him killed. When Columbo investigates the case, the mother is determined to make sure all his attention is on her and not her daughter, and an unlikely romance develops between sleuth and suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Faye Dunaway

programme.title: Columbo: Try and Catch Me (1977)
programme.desc: A grieving crime writer blames her niece's husband for her death and concocts a plan to take revenge, luring him to her walk-in vault, before locking him in and leaving him to die. She then puts her knowledge of the criminal mind to good use by covering her tracks, and although Columbo strongly suspects foul play, he finds clues are thin on the ground. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and Ruth Gordon

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Crucial Game (1972)
programme.desc: The owner of an American football team is found dead in a swimming pool, and his drowning is regarded as an accident. The down-at-heel detective is convinced that foul play was involved and sets out to prove his case - but his chief suspect has a cast-iron alibi that places him miles away from the scene of the crime. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Robert Culp

programme.title: Columbo: A Case of Immunity (1975)
programme.desc: A senior adviser to the king of a Middle Eastern nation plots to increase his own power by having the head of security at their American embassy killed and placing the blame on a group of dissidents. Columbo is assigned to crack the case, but the suspect's diplomatic immunity hinders the progress of the investigation. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Hector Elizondo and Sal Mineo

programme.title: Columbo: Death Lends a Hand (1971)
programme.desc: The crumpled cop investigates when a private eye murders his client's adulterous wife because she refuses to submit to his blackmail threats, and arranges the crime scene to make it look as if she was the victim of a mugging gone wrong. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ray Milland and Robert Culp

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is found raped and murdered in Central Park - and as her work involved contacting sexual partners of HIV patients, the detectives soon have a long list of suspects. The discovery of a second body raises fears that they are dealing with a serial killer - and the trail leads to a man who has no knowledge of the crimes, but is distantly connected to the first victim and her husband

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A rape investigation leads the team to a father and son who are being closely guarded under the witness protection programme so they can testify against Russian mobsters. To complicate matters, the victim is arrested on racketeering charges before it is possible to question her in detail. Pam Grier guest stars alongside Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The body of a badly beaten youth is found in the luggage compartment on an airport shuttle bus, leading investigators to make further grisly discoveries as a paedophile ring is exposed. Subsequent inquiries lead them to a travel agency specialising in exotic trips for sexual predators. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler are faced with the gruesome task of identifying an animal rights activist found decapitated in a park during a romantic rendezvous. The team try to find two potential killers who were in the vicinity - but are later stunned to discover the crime scene was staged to mislead investigators. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate the rape of a cellist, and initially believe her conductor is to blame for the attack - especially given his penchant for erotic film-making. However, they soon discover an obsessive stalker is watching her from afar - and will stop at nothing to continue his monitoring. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Dr Huang try to prove a convicted serial killer is responsible for the rape and murder of another woman, whose assailant was never identified. In doing so, they hope to give the victim's parents a sense of closure - but face a race against time as the man is due to be executed in three days. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, BD Wong and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Recently retired Alan and Jackie Patrick from Hampshire are keen to buy a property on the north of Spain's Costa Blanca in the Marina Alta region, close to where Jackie's brothers live. Ben Hillman show them a range of properties for their £85,000 budget, but will any character town houses have them reaching for their chequebook?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Creative Brighton couple Emily and Peter Harold have been holidaying on the Spanish island of Mallorca for 14 years, and are determined to buy a second home there. They're looking for somewhere to relax and soak up the sun and culture on offer in their favourite spot of Port de Soller. Laura Hamilton shows them five apartments for their £250,000 budge

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Denise Greenacre has retired and is determined to buy a home in Spain. Ben Hillman shows Denise and her daughter Rebecca five properties around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca for her £80,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Elaine and Nicos Nicolaou seek to return to their Greek roots by buying a property in Kefalonia, and Jasmine Harman seeks out five properties in the island's southern region that fall within the couple's £60,000 budget

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

Plus, footage of an armed robber who may be carrying a gun but still says 'please'

programme.title: Caught Red Handed

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A couple watch a break-in live on their mobile phones, then race to confront the burglar, and two crooks raid a museum, but face trouble when they try to spend the vintage loot

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: A sniffer dog picks out a suspicious suitcase at Gatwick and his handler discovers 3kg of cocaine inside, and two women are pulled over when returning from the Caribbean

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: Officers search a shipment of doors and discover a large concealment of drugs and ammunition destined for Devon, and in Bristol, the team receives a tip-off and pays a visit to suspected cigarette smugglers. Meanwhile, at Gatwick, staff catch a cocaine trafficker red-handed, and following a confession, set a trap for his accomplice

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: A passenger at Bristol Airport is caught smuggling cigarettes and admits to doing it for more than 10 years, and a suspicious bag is seized at Gatwick with £55,000 of cannabis inside. Plus, the crew of the fast cutter searcher investigates mysterious pot-markers off the Cornish coast

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: Customs officers at Bristol catch three passengers carrying 87kg of tobacco. Claiming the amount is for personal use they put up a fight when they learn they may not be allowed to keep it. The crew of the Searcher boards a yacht on the trail of drugs, and a sniffer dog picks up a scent at Gatwick that turns out to be cocaine. John Rand narrates

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: Off Cornwall, the crew of the fast cutter Searcher decides to board a lone yacht coming from the Canaries, a frequent transit point for cocaine. Officers in Dover detect a mysterious shadow in a truck full of chipboard, and in Bristol a man is caught carrying 5,000 cigarettes back from Turkey

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: In Dover, the specialist rummage team discovers a ton of cannabis hidden in a truck, and in Cornwall a ship's captain lies to officers of the cutter Searcher about the number of cigarettes he has on board. At Gatwick, a suspected heroin smuggler is subjected to an intimate search. Narrated by Jon Rand
