programme.title: The Secret Garden
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. The story of a spoiled little English girl who is orphaned in India and sent to live with her uncle where she discovers a garden

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks up The Great Orme in North Wales and beside a railway in West Yorkshire

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec moves into Zoe's flat with a framed picture of his idol Perry Mason

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec and Zoe find themselves separated when Valentine's Day arrives

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: LNYDP 21: London Welcomes You Back
programme.desc: London's New Year Day Parade throws a new year's party with a difference! Presented by Tim Vincent and Sinitta, parade performers from around the world gather for a Covid safe celebration

programme.title: Moby Dick (1956)
programme.desc: Obsessive skipper Captain Ahab leads his ship and crew on an epic quest to find and kill the legendary white whale that bit off his leg - but his lust for vengeance endangers all their lives. John Huston's seafaring adventure, based on Herman Melville's novel, starring Gregory Peck, Richard Baseheart, Orson Welles and Leo Genn

programme.title: Red Dog: The Early Years (2016)
programme.desc: A young boy shipped off to his grandad's remote cattle station finds friendship and adventure with a one-of-a-kind dog. Family drama, starring Levi Miller and Jason Isaacs

programme.title: I Was There: The Great War Interviews
programme.desc: In the early 1960s, the BBC spoke to almost 300 people about their experiences during the First World War for landmark series The Great War, but only a fraction of the interviews were broadcast. This programme airs a selection of the original material, most of which has never been screened before, with recollections of the horrors of artillery bombardment and fleeting outbreaks of peace on the battlefield, as well as the stories of those on the home front waiting for news of their loved ones. Narrated by Samuel West

programme.title: Diana: Last Days of a Princess
programme.desc: Genevieve O'Reilly and Patrick Baladi star as Diana, Princess of Wales and Dodi Al Fayed in a docu-drama charting the couple's last days, culminating in the fatal crash in the Alma Tunnel, Paris. Including interviews with Mohamed Al Fayed, bodyguard Kez Wingfield and masseuse Myriah Daniels

programme.title: Boy Soldiers of WWII
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the truth behind the boys who lied about their age in order to enlist and fight in the Second World War, and their shocking stories from the frontline

programme.title: Jack the Ripper
programme.desc: Part one of two. An alcoholic detective pursues the infamous serial killer brutally murdering prostitutes in Victorian London. Many potential suspects present themselves, from an anti-royal demagogue to an actor whose stage portrayal of murderous evil is a little too convincing, but the inspector is convinced a conspiracy seeks to protect the ripper's identity. Thriller, with Michael Caine, Jane Seymour, Lewis Collins and Harry Andrews

programme.title: The Krays - Mad Axeman (2019)
programme.desc: Based on the true story of Frank Mitchell who was sprung from Dartmoor Prison by the Krays 10 days before Christmas 1966. Whilst a national manhunt ensued, he was holed up in a flat in East London

programme.title: Dead Ringers (1988)
programme.desc: Identical twin gynaecologists Elliot and Beverly share everything in their life. Elliot, self-assured and experienced, passes his unwitting sexual conquests to shy and sensitive Beverly when he tires of them. However, when Beverly falls in love for the first time with a patient, the affair drives the brothers apart - and both men begin to lose their grip on their sanity. David Cronenberg's psychological thriller, with Jeremy Irons in a dual role alongside Genevieve Bujold

programme.title: Queen of the Blues (1979)
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a failing Soho nightclub and hope to turn its fortunes around - but they end up being threatened by gangsters demanding protection money. Fortunately, salvation is at hand in an enthusiastic stripper, who comes up with a plan to save the day. Drama, starring Mary Millington and Rosemary England

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Operation Third Form (1966)
programme.desc: A trio of youngsters tries to stop a gang of crooks and save an old junk collector from being accused of theft. Children's drama, starring Kevin Bennett and Sydney Bromley

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Ylvi and Ylva get stranded on a ledge in the middle of a cliff, but Vic builds a trampoline to save them

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar's crew participates in the Olympic games - and are determined to come out on top

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: A storm forces the girls' plane to land on a volcanic island - where they discover an old enemy has built a machine that controls the weather

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: An artificial intelligence tries to erase the band from the internet, while Maxine asks X to spy on the girls, as she is worried one of them is thinking about quitting

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of Sinbad the Sailor read by Rik Mayall

programme.title: A Little Princess
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. Sara, a kind and wealthy girl is left in boarding-school by her father. When her father dies the cruel, greedy Headmistress makes Sara the school slave

programme.title: The Boy Who Turned Yellow (1972)
programme.desc: A boy looking for his lost pet mouse has a strange experience on the London Underground that gives him special powers. Family adventure, starring Mark Dightam and Robert Eddison

programme.title: Rag Doll (1961)
programme.desc: A teenager runs away from her alcoholic stepfather to start a new life in London, only to fall under the influence of a seductive criminal, and get drawn into helping him rob her kindly boss. Crime drama, starring Christina Gregg, Jess Conrad and Hermione Baddeley

programme.title: In the Doghouse (1961)
programme.desc: A newly qualified vet struggles to compete against an unscrupulous rival. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Peggy Cummins, Hattie Jacques and James Booth

programme.title: Dear Murderer (1947)
programme.desc: A man believes he has committed the perfect crime when he kills his wife's two lovers in a jealous rage. Melodrama, starring Eric Portman, Dennis Price, Jack Warner, Greta Gynt and Hazel Court

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby

programme.title: Three Steps in the Dark (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy eccentric invites all the members of his family to his country house, so he can discuss the contents of his Will. However, when he is suddenly killed, everyone present is a suspect. Murder mystery, starring Greta Gynt and Hugh Sinclair

programme.title: Drive My Car
programme.desc: Short film parodying 1970s car adverts

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: First episode of the drama about a military bomb disposal team working in London during the blitz. New recruit Brian Ash is thrown in at the deep end. Starring Anthony Andrews

programme.title: Obsessed with My Body
programme.desc: With British boys now apparently spending 30 per cent more time in the bathroom than teenage girls and taking twice as many selfies, this documentary follows some of those in search of the perfect body. From bodybuilding and eyebrow waxing to make-up, the film also examines how far they will they go to achieve perfection. And what happens when things are taken to extremes?

programme.title: Jack the Ripper
programme.desc: Part two of two. An alcoholic detective pursues the infamous serial killer brutally murdering prostitutes in Victorian London. Many potential suspects present themselves, from an anti-royal demagogue to an actor whose stage portrayal of murderous evil is a little too convincing, but the inspector is convinced a conspiracy seeks to protect the ripper's identity. Thriller, with Michael Caine, Jane Seymour, Lewis Collins and Harry Andrews

programme.title: Brimstone and Treacle (1982)
programme.desc: Asinister stranger worms his way into a middle-aged couple's home by offering to take care of their comatose daughter - but it is only once he's part of the family that his true nature shines through. Drama based on Dennis Potter's TV play, starring Sting, Denholm Elliott, Joan Plowright and Suzanna Hamilton

programme.title: Music Within (2007)
programme.desc: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing-impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities. Biographical drama, starring Ron Livingston, Melissa George and Michael Sheen

programme.title: These Foolish Things (2005)
programme.desc: A young woman dreams of following in her mother's footsteps and becoming an actress, but along the way becomes involved in a love triangle with a charming director and a penniless playwright. Romantic drama set in the 1940s, starring Zoe Tapper, Anjelica Huston, Lauren Bacall and Terence Stamp

programme.title: Drive My Car
programme.desc: Short film parodying 1970s car adverts

programme.title: Rockets in the Dunes (1960)
programme.desc: A Devon beach is used by local children for racing sand yachts, but their fun may be at an end when the army plan to use the dunes as a training ground. The youngsters rally the local community to save their beach, but the discovery of disused mines complicates things further. Family drama, starring Richard Adam and Fiona Davie

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks from Banavie to Gairlocky and around the Ribblehead Viaduct

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: Charlton Heston Presents the Bible
programme.desc: The renowned actor serves as storyteller an ancient Roman amphitheatre and other locales in Israel provide the settings, bringing the King James text to life

programme.title: Buttons: A Christmas Tale (2018)
programme.desc: An orphan facing the propspect of life on the street receives a visit from a guardian angel, who comforts her with the story of another child's search for a family and the role another mysterious helper played in her life. Children's fantasy, starring Angela Lansbury and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Hell is Sold Out (1951)
programme.desc: An author long assumed dead makes a surprise return, only to find books he did not write are being published under his name. He discovers that another writer, claiming to be his widow, is passing off her own creations as his previously unseen work to get them published. Drama, starring Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom and Richard Attenborough

programme.title: Angels One Five (1953)
programme.desc: A new recruit joins a squadron of British fighter pilots in the summer of 1940, as they prepare to face a confrontation with the overwhelming forces of the Luftwaffe over the English Channel, while the ground crew at their base anxiously awaits reports from the conflict. Second World War adventure, starring Jack Hawkins and John Gregson

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: Author and entrepreneur Michael Hayman interviews the business leaders at the heart of London's most exciting success stories

programme.title: Obsessed with My Body
programme.desc: With British boys now apparently spending 30 per cent more time in the bathroom than teenage girls and taking twice as many selfies, this documentary follows some of those in search of the perfect body. From bodybuilding and eyebrow waxing to make-up, the film also examines how far they will they go to achieve perfection. And what happens when things are taken to extremes?

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash employs some new technology that does not work. A new recruit gets some training, and a magazine article causes tension amid the team's superior officers

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: With David Attenborough. A heron's and eel's eye view of the Thames, once deadly to swim in, now alive with ducks, meadow flowers and rare dragonflies

programme.title: A London Community in Lockdown
programme.desc: A heartfelt look at life during lockdown in the Listraban community in London, a record of a moment in time that will be in the history books for generations to come

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Record producer Sam Phillips recalls how he discovered Elvis Presley. Landmark series from the 1970s, recounting the birth of rock 'n' roll

programme.title: Away (2016)
programme.desc: In Blackpool, an unlikely friendship blossoms between a lonely, suicidal widower and a young woman trying to escape an abusive ex-boyfriend. Drama, starring Timothy Spall, Juno Temple and Matt Ryan

programme.title: Dead Ringers (1988)
programme.desc: Identical twin gynaecologists Elliot and Beverly share everything in their life. Elliot, self-assured and experienced, passes his unwitting sexual conquests to shy and sensitive Beverly when he tires of them. However, when Beverly falls in love for the first time with a patient, the affair drives the brothers apart - and both men begin to lose their grip on their sanity. David Cronenberg's psychological thriller, with Jeremy Irons in a dual role alongside Genevieve Bujold

programme.title: Strippers vs Werewolves (2012)
programme.desc: Lapdancer Justice kills a customer when he undergoes a terrifying transformation, and the body is quietly disposed of. The dead man was a werewolf, and at the next full moon, the other members of his pack plan to seek revenge. Justice and her fellow strippers have one month to prepare themselves for a battle to the death. Comedy horror, starring Adele Silva and Robert Englund

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Pretentious Alan, violent Kix and sex-mad Eleanor are part of a 1960s group of poets, artists and angry young men who convene for a weekend of amusement at a large villa overlooking the sea. programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner
